i-mobile.description: i-mobile is a advertising platform for clients to advertise their product and for publishers to monetize their cyberspace.
i-mobile.history: i-mobile is a Japanese digital advertising network founded in the early 2000s, operating as an ad platform for Japanese publishers and advertisers. It focuses on the Japanese domestic market.
i-mobile.summary_abuse: i-mobile is a Japanese ad network. Like most ad platforms, it can be a vector for malvertising targeting Japanese users, but it operates primarily within the Japanese domestic market and has no significant global abuse profile.

i-motor.description: i-motor is a platform that enhances automotive dealer websites by providing innovative, connected solutions to improve the online experience.
i-motor.history: i-motor is an Australian automotive dealer website platform providing digital solutions to car dealerships; no notable ownership changes or deprecation events are documented.
i-motor.summary_abuse: i-motor is an automotive dealer website platform with no notable abuse signal; it serves licensed car dealerships with no anonymous public signup.

i-MSCP.description: i-MSCP (internet Multi Server Control Panel) is a software for shared hosting environments management on Linux servers.
i-MSCP.history: i-MSCP (internet Multi Server Control Panel) is an open-source shared hosting control panel for Linux servers, developed as a community project around 2010; it has seen declining activity in recent years and appears largely dormant.
i-MSCP.summary_abuse: i-MSCP is a self-hosted hosting control panel with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

i30con.description: i30con is an icon toolkit based on CSS and JavaScript.
i30con.history: i30con is a CSS and JavaScript icon toolkit associated with 30nama.com; it appears to be a small niche icon library with no notable ownership changes or founding history found in public records.
i30con.summary_abuse: i30con is a frontend icon toolkit with no consumer-facing hosted surface. No documented abuse signal exists for this library.

iAdvize.description: iAdvize is a conversational marketing platform that connects customers in need of advice with experts who are available 24/7 via messaging.
iAdvize.history: iAdvize was founded in 2010 in Nantes, France, as a conversational marketing platform connecting website visitors with product experts. The company raised over EUR 32 million in VC funding including rounds from Bpifrance and Idinvest Partners and expanded internationally, focusing on AI-assisted messaging for enterprise customer support.
iAdvize.summary_abuse: iAdvize is a B2B live chat and conversational marketing platform with no notable abuse signal. Its enterprise-only audience, contact-sales pricing, and focus on brand-verified customer support channels give fraudsters no practical surface to exploit.

Iamport.description: Iamport is an information technology company based in South Korea.
Iamport.history: Iamport was founded in 2015 in Seoul, South Korea, as a payment gateway API enabling Korean e-commerce developers to integrate multiple payment processors. The company later became associated with PortOne, a Singapore-based global payments infrastructure company that rebranded and expanded the service.
Iamport.summary_abuse: Iamport is a legitimate payment gateway API primarily serving Korean e-commerce merchants. Payment gateways are not typically abused directly, though they can be targeted by fraudulent merchant accounts attempting to process unauthorized transactions. No specific incidents of abuse involving Iamport have been publicly documented.

Ibexa DXP.description: Ibexa DXP is an open-source enterprise PHP content management system (CMS) and Digital Experience Platform (DXP) developed by the company Ibexa (known previously as eZ Systems).
Ibexa DXP.history: Ibexa DXP originated as eZ Systems, a Norwegian company founded in 1999 that created the eZ Publish CMS. The company rebranded to Ibexa in 2021 and repositioned its platform as a Digital Experience Platform. The project has been open-source since its early days.
Ibexa DXP.summary_abuse: Ibexa DXP is an enterprise CMS with no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted or managed enterprise platform used by large organizations and has no consumer-facing free tier.

IBM Carbon Design System.description: IBM Carbon Design System is an open-source design system for building products and digital experiences.
IBM Carbon Design System.history: IBM Carbon Design System was created by IBM and open-sourced around 2016-2017. It is a comprehensive design system and component library used across IBM's digital products and widely adopted in enterprise frontend development. IBM actively maintains the project on GitHub.
IBM Carbon Design System.summary_abuse: IBM Carbon Design System is an open-source UI component library from IBM with no abuse signal. It is a frontend developer tool with no consumer-facing hosted surface.

IBM Coremetrics.history: Coremetrics was founded in 1999 in San Mateo, California as a cloud-based web analytics platform serving retail and e-commerce companies. IBM acquired Coremetrics in August 2010 for an undisclosed sum to expand its analytics capabilities. Post-acquisition, Coremetrics was rebranded as IBM Digital Analytics and integrated into IBM's marketing solutions portfolio. The product was eventually discontinued as IBM shifted analytics investment to Cognos and Watson.
IBM Coremetrics.summary_abuse: IBM Coremetrics (later IBM Digital Analytics) is a discontinued enterprise web analytics platform with no notable abuse signals. As an enterprise B2B analytics tool used by major retailers, it had no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited for abuse. IBM maintains comprehensive security practices across its portfolio.

IBM DataPower.description: IBM DataPower Gateway is a single multi-channel gateway designed to help provide security, control, integration and optimized access to a full range of mobile, web, application programming interface (API), service-oriented architecture (SOA), B2B and cloud workloads.
IBM DataPower.history: IBM DataPower was originally developed by DataPower Technology, founded in 1999, and acquired by IBM in 2005. It provides hardware and software XML and API gateway appliances for enterprise integration and security. IBM later introduced a cloud-native version.
IBM DataPower.summary_abuse: IBM DataPower Gateway is an enterprise API and integration gateway used in large financial, government, and healthcare organizations. It is not a consumer-facing product and has no abuse signal.

IBM HTTP Server.history: IBM HTTP Server is based on Apache HTTP Server and has been part of IBM's WebSphere Application Server middleware stack since the late 1990s. IBM bundled and customized Apache for enterprise Java workloads. It is primarily used in legacy enterprise environments running IBM middleware.
IBM HTTP Server.summary_abuse: IBM HTTP Server is an enterprise web server used in large IBM middleware environments. It runs on private infrastructure within enterprise data centers and has no consumer-facing abuse surface.

ICE.description: ICE is integrated customer engagement software designed to streamline communication, support, and interaction across multiple channels for businesses.
ICE.history: ICE (Integrated Customer Engagement) is a live chat and customer engagement platform by Enterprise Technology Solutions available at enterice.com. The platform targets businesses needing browser-based live chat with SSL-encrypted communications. It is a niche product with limited public history and no documented ownership changes.
ICE.summary_abuse: ICE is a small live chat and CRM platform with no notable abuse signals. As a B2B customer engagement tool embedded on business websites, its abuse surface is limited. No public incidents or security research reports against this platform are documented.

Icegram.description: Icegram is a WordPress plugin designed to grow subscribers and increase conversions.
Icegram.history: Icegram was launched in 2014 by Leap Ventures, an India-based company, as a WordPress plugin suite for email opt-in popups, CTAs, and lead generation. The product has grown into a family of plugins (Icegram Engage, Icegram Express, Icegram Collect) targeting small WordPress site owners.
Icegram.summary_abuse: Icegram is a WordPress popup and lead capture plugin with no notable abuse profile. Its functionality for collecting email addresses could theoretically be misconfigured by site owners, but the plugin itself is a standard marketing tool with no history of being weaponized or associated with spam campaigns.

iCheck.description: iCheck is a library for customizable checkboxes and radio buttons.
iCheck.history: iCheck is a jQuery and Zepto plugin for customizable checkboxes and radio buttons, released around 2013 by Fronteed. It was widely adopted but development has been largely inactive for several years.
iCheck.summary_abuse: iCheck is a client-side UI library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

iCIMS.description: iCIMS is a talent acquisition and internal mobility platform that delivers solutions to build, retain, and scale your workforce.
iCIMS.history: iCIMS was founded in 2000 by Colin Day as a talent acquisition SaaS platform. It grew into a major enterprise ATS provider, was acquired by Vista Equity Partners in August 2018, and TA Associates joined as co-investor in 2022. It serves thousands of enterprise customers including a significant share of the Fortune 500.
iCIMS.summary_abuse: iCIMS is an enterprise talent acquisition platform with no documented abuse cases. Its B2B-only, enterprise-gated model and established security posture mean it presents no meaningful abuse risk.

iClose.description: iClose is an appointment scheduling tool that helps businesses streamline lead generation and meeting coordination without additional advertising expenses.
iClose.history: iClosed is an AI-powered appointment scheduling and CRM platform launched around 2022-2023 by Ismael Esn and Jahanzaib Sohail, targeting high-ticket sales teams. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes since founding.
iClose.summary_abuse: iClosed has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche B2B appointment scheduling tool for sales teams with no consumer-facing content hosting features that would attract scammers.

icomm.description: icomm is a platform that helps companies manage their marketing automation strategy using AI-powered tools.
icomm.history: Founded in 2006 in Buenos Aires, Argentina, icomm is a marketing automation platform serving over 1,300 clients across 12 countries in Latin America and Spain. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes have occurred.
icomm.summary_abuse: icomm is an email and marketing automation platform serving B2B clients in Latin America. As an email marketing tool it could theoretically be used for spam, but icomm is a managed service targeting known business clients with no documented pattern of abuse or misuse.

icomm - Notifications Hub.description: Notifications Hub is an omnichannel marketing automation platform powered by AI.0
icomm - Notifications Hub.history: icomm Notifications Hub is an omnichannel marketing automation product by icomm, a company founded in 2006 in Buenos Aires, Argentina, serving clients across Latin America and Spain.
icomm - Notifications Hub.summary_abuse: icomm Notifications Hub is a managed B2B omnichannel messaging platform. As a push notification and email tool it could be misused for spam, but it is a business service with no documented pattern of abuse.

Iconosquare.description: Iconosquare is an all-in-one social media management platform that facilitates the lives of SMMs with multiple profiles to manage.
Iconosquare.history: Iconosquare was founded in 2011 by Jérôme Boudot in Limoges, France, originally as Statigram, a free Instagram analytics tool. It rebranded to Iconosquare in 2014 and expanded into a full social media management platform covering Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, and Twitter analytics and scheduling.
Iconosquare.summary_abuse: Iconosquare is a legitimate social media management and analytics platform with no notable abuse history. The service is oriented toward marketing professionals and brands, requires account authentication, and has no features that could meaningfully enable phishing or spam operations.

IconScout.description: IconScout is a platform that offers a diverse collection of high-quality icons and design resources for creative professionals, allowing them to enhance their projects with visually appealing elements.
IconScout.history: IconScout was founded in 2016 by Dalpat Prajapati in Surat, India, as a marketplace for design assets including icons, illustrations, and Lottie animations. In February 2021, the company was acquired by LottieFiles, a platform specializing in lightweight animation files.
IconScout.summary_abuse: IconScout is a design asset marketplace with no notable abuse profile. As a content hosting platform, it could theoretically host infringing assets, but the vendor actively curates content. There are no documented incidents of the platform being used for phishing, malware, or other malicious purposes.

Icordis CMS.description: Icordis CMS is a proprietary content management system developed by LCP nv for managing custom business and institutional websites.
Icordis CMS.history: Icordis CMS is a proprietary content management system developed by LCP nv, a Belgian IT services company. It is designed for managing custom business websites and intranets, primarily serving the Belgian market.
Icordis CMS.summary_abuse: Icordis CMS is a niche Belgian enterprise CMS with no known abuse profile. It serves a small set of business clients with custom deployments and has no public-facing consumer surface that could be exploited for phishing or spam operations.

ID5.description: ID5 is a company that offers an identity solution for digital advertising, providing a Universal ID that enables privacy-compliant user recognition and tracking across websites without relying on personal information or third-party cookies.
ID5.history: ID5 was founded in 2017 in London by Mathieu Roche and Pierre-Antoine Durgeat as a cookieless universal identifier for digital advertising. It raised a $20M Series B and in November 2025 acquired TrueData to expand its identity coverage.
ID5.summary_abuse: ID5 is an adtech identity resolution service that tracks users across sites using hashed emails, IPs, and ML models for advertising targeting. Its cross-site tracking is by design and may raise privacy concerns for end-users, though the company claims GDPR compliance and consent-based operation. No specific fraud or malware abuse patterns are documented.

idCloudHost.description: idCloudHost is a local web service provider based in Indonesia that offer a wide range of services including domain name registration and cloud hosting.
idCloudHost.history: idCloudHost is an Indonesian web hosting and cloud services provider offering domain registration, shared hosting, VPS, and related services to the Indonesian market.
idCloudHost.summary_abuse: idCloudHost is an Indonesian hosting provider. Shared hosting providers can host abusive content, but no specific incidents involving idCloudHost are documented.

Ideasoft.description: Ideasoft is a Turkish software company providing web-based software solutions, software design, ecommerce solutions, and other services.
Ideasoft.history: IdeaSoft was founded in 2005 in Turkey as an e-commerce software provider. It has grown to power over 45,000 Turkish e-commerce websites, making it one of the leading domestic e-commerce platforms in the Turkish market.
Ideasoft.summary_abuse: IdeaSoft is a legitimate Turkish e-commerce platform with no notable abuse profile. As an e-commerce infrastructure provider, its stores could be used for fraudulent merchant activity, but this is common to all e-commerce platforms. No specific incidents involving IdeaSoft's platform being weaponized have been documented.

Identixweb iCart.description: Identixweb iCart is a Shopify upsell app designed to boost pre-purchase sales and conversions directly in the cart without disrupting the shopping experience.
Identixweb iCart.history: Identixweb iCart is a Shopify app developed by Identixweb, an India-based Shopify development agency. The iCart app provides in-cart upsell and cross-sell functionality for Shopify merchants to increase average order value.
Identixweb iCart.summary_abuse: Identixweb iCart is a Shopify cart optimization app with no notable abuse profile. It is a standard e-commerce conversion tool operating within the Shopify ecosystem, which has its own review and compliance processes. No abuse incidents have been associated with this specific app.

Identrust.description: denTrust is a digital identity authentication solution.
Identrust.history: IdenTrust was founded in 1996 and is a well-established digital certificate authority providing identity authentication certificates for financial institutions, healthcare, and government sectors. It is particularly known for signing Let's Encrypt's root certificate, which underpinned much of the web's free TLS infrastructure.
Identrust.summary_abuse: IdenTrust is a trusted Certificate Authority with a long operating history in financial and government identity management. It has no notable abuse signal. Its certificates are used for secure authentication in regulated industries.

Ideta.description: Ideta is a platform that provides chatbots and callbots to streamline company operations through AI and automation.
Ideta.history: Ideta was founded in 2017 in France as a no-code chatbot and callbot platform. It raised €1.7 million in seed funding and positioned itself as a multi-AI platform supporting models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Mistral for enterprise workflow automation.
Ideta.summary_abuse: Ideta is a no-code chatbot platform with no known abuse profile. As a business automation tool, it requires registration and is not a consumer-facing service. No incidents of the platform being exploited for spam, phishing, or malware distribution have been reported.

IdoSell Shop.description: IdoSell Shop is a fully functional ecommerce software platform.
IdoSell Shop.history: IdoSell was founded in 2000 in Szczecin, Poland, by IAI sp. z o.o. It has grown to become one of Poland's leading e-commerce platforms, listed on the NewConnect stock exchange since 2009. The company also operates IdoBooking, a hotel management and booking system.
IdoSell Shop.summary_abuse: IdoSell is a legitimate Polish e-commerce platform with no notable abuse profile. As a cloud-based shop platform, it serves vetted merchants and maintains Polish and EU regulatory compliance. No incidents of systematic abuse have been associated with the platform.

iEntry.description: iEntry is a full-service email marketing platform offering campaign management, list building, analytics, and targeted messaging for businesses.
iEntry.history: iEntry is a US-based email marketing platform that has operated since the early 2000s, providing campaign management and list-building tools for publishers and businesses.
iEntry.summary_abuse: iEntry is an email marketing platform. Email platforms carry inherent spam risk; iEntry's older infrastructure and niche status suggest limited abuse monitoring compared to mainstream ESPs.

iEXExchanger.history: iEXExchanger is a self-hosted cryptocurrency exchange script built on PHP/Laravel and Angular by the iExBase team, sold as a licensed software product for operators to launch their own crypto exchange offices. The platform has around 15 known live deployments globally.
iEXExchanger.summary_abuse: iEXExchanger is a script used to operate cryptocurrency exchange offices. Crypto exchanger software inherently carries elevated abuse risk as some operators may facilitate money laundering, scam proceeds, or unregulated transactions. The script itself is a self-hosted product, so abuse depends largely on how individual operators deploy and regulate their instances. No documented vendor-level anti-abuse enforcement exists.

IFC Markets.description: IFC Markets is a provider of Forex and CFD trading.
IFC Markets.history: IFC Markets is an online Forex and CFD broker offering retail trading; minimal verifiable history about founding year is publicly available.
IFC Markets.summary_abuse: IFC Markets is a Forex and CFD broker operating in a sector with elevated fraud risk. Retail FX/CFD brokers are a common vehicle for trading scams, fake investments, and unauthorized cloning by scammers impersonating regulated brokers. The platform targets retail traders, a demographic frequently targeted by investment fraud.

Ifdo.description: Ifdo is a marketing and segmentation tool
Ifdo.history: Ifdo is a Korean marketing and customer segmentation tool. Limited public information is available about its founding history or company structure.
Ifdo.summary_abuse: Ifdo is a niche Korean marketing segmentation tool with no known abuse profile. The platform serves business clients for marketing automation purposes and has no public-facing consumer surface relevant to phishing or spam abuse.

Iframely.description: Iframely is a uniform delivery service for rich media embeds and URL metadata across various platforms.
Iframely.history: Iframely was started in 2012 to standardize rich media embedding across the web. The open-source core is available on GitHub (itteco/iframely) and supports over 1,900 media publishers. The service provides both a hosted API and a self-hosted option for developers needing URL metadata and oEmbed responses.
Iframely.summary_abuse: Iframely is a URL metadata and media embed API with no notable abuse profile. The service fetches and normalizes metadata from legitimate media publishers and does not host user content. No documented incidents of the platform being abused for phishing or malware delivery.

Ignition.description: Ignition is an all-in-one platform streamlining proposals, billing, payments, and workflows for accounting and professional services firms.
Ignition.history: Ignition (formerly Practice Ignition) was founded in 2013 in Sydney, Australia, as a proposal and billing automation platform for accountants and professional services firms. It rebranded to Ignition around 2021 to reflect its broader applicability beyond accounting practices.
Ignition.summary_abuse: Ignition is a professional services billing and proposal platform with no notable abuse profile. The service targets accounting firms and professional service agencies, requires verified business accounts, and has no consumer-facing surface susceptible to phishing or spam abuse.

iGoDigital.description: iGoDigital provides web-based commerce tools, personalisation, and product recommendations designed to increase customer interaction.
iGoDigital.history: iGoDigital was founded in 2004 and built a web personalization and product recommendation platform. In October 2012 it was acquired by ExactTarget for approximately $21 million to deepen ExactTarget's web capabilities with predictive analytics. ExactTarget was itself acquired by Salesforce in 2013, making iGoDigital a deep subsidiary of Salesforce, where its technology was absorbed into the Marketing Cloud suite.
iGoDigital.summary_abuse: iGoDigital is a legacy B2B personalization platform folded into Salesforce Marketing Cloud with no notable abuse signal. Its enterprise-only deployment model and absorption into a major cloud suite leave no consumer-facing surface for fraudsters.

iHomefinder IDX.description: iHomefinder provides IDX property search, built-in CRM, and marketing tools.
iHomefinder IDX.history: iHomefinder was established in 2001 in Berkeley, California, as a provider of IDX (Internet Data Exchange) real estate search technology and websites for real estate agents. In May 2017, FRONTSTEPS (formerly Real Pro Systems) completed the acquisition of iHomefinder, integrating the two platforms under the iHomefinder brand. iHomefinder continues to operate as a subsidiary of FRONTSTEPS, offering IDX property search, CRM, and lead generation tools for real estate professionals.
iHomefinder IDX.summary_abuse: iHomefinder is a real estate IDX search and CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used by licensed real estate agents and brokers, requiring professional registration. No documented phishing, fraud, or scam incidents are associated with iHomefinder.

iiQ Check.description: iiQ Check is a system designed to optimize hotel guest communication and manage review distribution.
iiQ Check.history: iiQ Check is a German SaaS platform for hotel guest communication and review management, targeted at the hospitality industry. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes documented.
iiQ Check.summary_abuse: iiQ Check has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche B2B hotel review management tool with no consumer-facing hosting features that attract fraud or scams.

IIS.description: Internet Information Services (IIS) is an extensible web server software created by Microsoft for use with the Windows NT family.
IIS.history: Internet Information Services (IIS) was first released by Microsoft in 1995 as part of Windows NT 3.51. It became one of the world's most widely deployed web servers, bundled with Windows Server. IIS has undergone continuous development through numerous versions, adding support for modern protocols and ASP.NET.
IIS.summary_abuse: IIS is Microsoft's web server, commonly found on Windows-based hosting infrastructure. While IIS itself is not abused, sites running IIS are subject to the same threats as any public web server, including exploitation of unpatched vulnerabilities. Historic IIS vulnerabilities like Code Red (2001) caused widespread compromise. Modern IIS is well-maintained and not specifically targeted more than other web servers.

Ikas.description: Ikas is a new generation ecommerce platform designed for small businesses.
Ikas.history: Ikas was founded in 2017 in Turkey as a next-generation e-commerce platform targeting small and medium businesses. In April 2024, the company raised $20 million in Series A funding to expand its omnichannel retail management platform across Turkey and international markets.
Ikas.summary_abuse: Ikas is a legitimate Turkish e-commerce SaaS platform with no notable abuse history. As an e-commerce infrastructure provider, merchant stores on the platform could theoretically be used for fraud, but this is standard risk across all e-commerce platforms. No documented incidents of the Ikas platform being systematically abused.

Ikeono.description: Ikeono is an all-in-one communication tool that provides business text messaging solutions for independent retailers.
Ikeono.history: Ikeono is a business text messaging and communication tool designed for independent retailers and specialty stores. The platform integrates with point-of-sale systems to enable SMS-based customer communication for order updates and support.
Ikeono.summary_abuse: Ikeono is a business SMS communication platform targeting independent retailers with no known abuse profile. The service enables legitimate business-to-customer text messaging and is not a consumer-facing platform susceptible to spam or phishing abuse.

ikiwiki.description: ikiwiki is a free and open-source wiki application.
ikiwiki.history: ikiwiki is a free, open-source wiki and blog compiler created by Joey Hess, first released in 2006. It compiles wiki pages to static HTML and is stored in a version control system. Development has slowed considerably in recent years.
html: <link rel="alternate" type="application/x-wiki" title="Edit this page" href="[^"]*/ikiwiki\.cgi
ikiwiki.summary_abuse: ikiwiki is a self-hosted static wiki generator with no consumer-facing hosted surface and no documented abuse signal.

Ilias.description: Ilias is an open source learning management system.
Ilias.history: ILIAS is an open-source LMS first released in 1998 by the University of Cologne in Germany. It is widely used in German-speaking universities and corporate training environments. It is governed by the ILIAS Society, a nonprofit association.
Ilias.summary_abuse: ILIAS is a self-hosted open-source learning management system. It has no consumer-facing hosted service and no notable abuse signal. It is deployed by educational institutions and enterprises on their own infrastructure.

Iluria.description: Iluria is a hosted ecommerce platform from Brazil.
Iluria.history: Iluria is a Brazilian hosted e-commerce platform that emerged around 2012, targeting small businesses and independent brands in Brazil. It generated approximately $2.7 million in annual revenue with a small team, serving Brazilian merchants in fashion, design, and boutique retail.
Iluria.summary_abuse: Iluria is a legitimate Brazilian e-commerce platform with no notable abuse profile. As an online store hosting service, it could theoretically be used by bad actors to create fraudulent shops, but this risk is standard across all hosted e-commerce platforms. No documented incidents of systematic abuse involving Iluria have been reported.

iMagaza.description: iMagaza is an ecommerce website system from Turkey.
iMagaza.history: iMagaza (iMağaza Eticaret) was founded in 2010 in Istanbul, Turkey. The company provides customizable ecommerce website packages for small and medium-sized businesses in Turkey, including marketplace, shipping, and accounting integrations. The platform primarily serves the Turkish ecommerce market.
iMagaza.summary_abuse: iMagaza is a Turkish ecommerce platform with a modest abuse risk profile. Ecommerce platforms in Turkey can be used to set up counterfeit or fraudulent storefronts, though no specific incidents are documented for iMagaza. The platform's limited public profile makes it difficult to assess the vendor's anti-abuse posture.

Image Placeholders.description: Performance plugin from the WordPress Performance Team, which determines and stores the dominant color for newly uploaded images in the media library, then uses that as a placeholder background during front end image loads.
Image Placeholders.history: Image Placeholders (Dominant Color Images) is a WordPress performance plugin developed by the WordPress Performance Team, an official working group within the WordPress open-source project. It was introduced around 2022 as part of WordPress's core performance initiative.
Image Placeholders.summary_abuse: Image Placeholders is a WordPress performance plugin from the official WordPress Performance Team. It has no abuse signal and serves a legitimate performance optimization use case.

Image Prioritizer.description: Performance plugin from the WordPress Performance Team, which optimizes the loading of images which are the LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) element, including both img elements and elements with CSS background images.
Image Prioritizer.history: Image Prioritizer is a WordPress performance plugin from the WordPress Performance Team, introduced around 2023. It optimizes the loading of Largest Contentful Paint images to improve Core Web Vitals scores.
Image Prioritizer.summary_abuse: Image Prioritizer is a WordPress performance plugin from the official WordPress Performance Team. It has no abuse signal and serves a legitimate performance optimization use case.

Image Relay.description: Image Relay is a digital asset management system that allows organizations to upload, manage, organize, monitor and track their digital assets.
Image Relay.history: Image Relay was founded in 2002 in Burlington, Vermont, as a digital asset management platform for businesses to organize and distribute brand assets. In September 2024, the company was acquired by Canto, a leading DAM platform, and the Image Relay brand was folded into Canto.
Image Relay.summary_abuse: Image Relay is a digital asset management platform with no notable abuse profile. The service stores and distributes brand assets for enterprise clients, requiring authenticated access. There is no consumer-facing surface or feature set that would make it a target for phishing or spam campaigns.

ImageEngine.description: ImageEngine is an intelligent image content delivery network. ImageEngine will resize your image content tailored to the end users device.
ImageEngine.history: ImageEngine was launched in 2015 by ScientiaMobile, a Virginia-based company known for the WURFL device detection library. It provides an intelligent image CDN that automatically detects device capabilities and resizes images accordingly, reducing page weight for end users.
ImageEngine.summary_abuse: ImageEngine is an image CDN with no notable abuse profile. As infrastructure for delivering optimized images, it does not host user-generated content in any form that could facilitate phishing or spam. Access requires authentication, and no consumer-facing surface exists.

Imagekit.description: ImageKit is an image optimisation and transformation server for web and mobile app developers.
Imagekit.history: ImageKit was founded in 2017 in India as an image optimization and transformation CDN for web and mobile developers. It has raised venture funding and grown as a popular image management API service.
Imagekit.summary_abuse: ImageKit is an image optimization and CDN service. It is a technical API layer for image delivery and has no consumer-facing content hosting. No notable abuse signal is associated with this technology.

Imagely NextGEN Gallery.description: NextGEN Gallery is a WordPress gallery plugin maintained by Imagely.
Imagely NextGEN Gallery.history: NextGEN Gallery is one of the oldest and most popular WordPress gallery plugins, originally created by Alex Rabe and later maintained by Imagely. The plugin has accumulated over 1.5 million active installations and is a long-standing staple of the WordPress photography and portfolio website ecosystem.
Imagely NextGEN Gallery.summary_abuse: NextGEN Gallery by Imagely is a widely-used WordPress gallery plugin with no notable abuse profile. As a self-hosted WordPress plugin for displaying images, it has no consumer-facing service surface. No documented incidents of the plugin being used for malicious purposes.

imagesLoaded.description: jQuery plugin for seeing if the images are loaded.
imagesLoaded.history: imagesLoaded is a JavaScript library created by David DeSandro for detecting when images finish loading. It has been a widely used utility since around 2011.
imagesLoaded.summary_abuse: imagesLoaded is a client-side JavaScript utility with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

Imba.description: Imba is a Web programming language that compiles to performant JavaScript, providing language-level support for defining, extending, subclassing, and instantiating.
Imba.history: Imba is a programming language that compiles to JavaScript, originally developed by Sindre Aarsaether. It focuses on building reactive web interfaces with minimal overhead, offering language-level support for DOM manipulation and reactivity.
Imba.summary_abuse: Imba is a compile-to-JavaScript programming language with no abuse signal. It is a developer tool with no consumer-facing hosted service.

Imber.description: Imber is an all-in-one marketing automation platform built for customer support (live chat), sales, and marketing.
Imber.history: Imber is an all-in-one marketing automation and live chat platform for customer support, sales, and marketing. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Imber.summary_abuse: Imber is a live chat and marketing automation SaaS with no documented abuse signal.

ImBox.description: Imbox is a live chat platform that includes co-browsing features.
ImBox.history: ImBox is a Swedish live chat and co-browsing platform primarily serving Nordic businesses. The service provides real-time customer support tools including co-browsing capabilities that allow support agents to view customers' browser sessions.
ImBox.summary_abuse: ImBox is a Swedish live chat platform with no notable abuse profile. It provides authenticated business-to-customer communication tools and does not offer anonymous consumer-facing services that could be exploited for phishing or spam operations.

Imgix.description: Imgix is a visual media platform for managing, processing, rendering, optimising and delivering your existing images.
Imgix.history: Imgix was founded in 2011 and went through Y Combinator. It became a leading real-time image processing and delivery CDN, generating approximately $10.4 million in annual revenue with 59 employees as of 2024. The platform serves engineering teams at major consumer and enterprise websites.
Imgix.summary_abuse: Imgix is an image processing CDN infrastructure platform with no notable abuse profile. As a developer-facing API requiring authentication and billing setup, it has no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited for phishing or content hosting abuse.

immediaCMS.description: immediaCMS is a content management system that enables users to create, edit, and organize digital content through a centralized platform for website and media management.
immediaCMS.history: immediaCMS is a content management system provided by immediac.com for website and media management. Very limited public history is available.
immediaCMS.summary_abuse: immediaCMS has no notable abuse signal. It is a small niche CMS product with limited public presence and no documented abuse history.

Immerss.description: Immerss is a platform that offers 1-to-1 Digital Clienteling, allowing customers to connect with businesses in real time via dynamic video, chat, and messaging for a personalized shopping experience.
Immerss.history: Immerss is a digital clienteling and live shopping platform enabling one-to-one video connections between customers and retail associates. It targets luxury and specialty retailers seeking to replicate in-store consultation experiences online.
Immerss.summary_abuse: Immerss is a niche live video clienteling platform for retail with no known abuse profile. The service connects shoppers with authenticated retail associates and does not offer any consumer-facing hosting or anonymous access features that could be exploited for fraud.

Immutable.js.description: Immutable.js is a JavaScript library developed by Facebook that provides immutable data structures for more predictable state management in applications.
Immutable.js.history: Immutable.js was created by Facebook (now Meta) and first released in 2014. It provides immutable data structures for JavaScript, commonly used with React and Redux applications. The project is maintained by Meta and the open-source community.
Immutable.js.summary_abuse: Immutable.js is a JavaScript library for immutable data structures. It has no consumer-facing hosted service and no abuse signal. It is a foundational development tool used in JavaScript application state management.

Impact.description: Impact helps businesses contract and pay partners.
Impact.history: Impact (impact.com) was founded in 2008 and has grown into one of the leading affiliate and partnership management platforms. It provides tools for brands to manage affiliate, influencer, and B2B partner programs at enterprise scale.
Impact.summary_abuse: Impact.com is an affiliate and partnership management platform. Affiliate marketing platforms can be exploited for commission fraud, fake traffic, and coupon stacking abuse. Impact serves enterprise clients under contracts with fraud detection tools, but affiliate networks are inherently susceptible to abuse by bad-faith publishers. No specific large-scale incident is documented for Impact itself.

imperia CMS.description: imperia CMS is a headless content management for large editorial.
imperia CMS.history: imperia CMS is a German enterprise content management system with roots dating to the mid-1990s. It is developed and sold by pirobase imperia GmbH and serves large editorial teams primarily in Germany and the DACH region.
imperia CMS.summary_abuse: imperia CMS is an enterprise CMS with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse cases.

Impero.description: Impero offers educational and corporate customers the most secure way of remotely accessing devices.
Impero.history: Impero Software was founded in 2002 in Nottingham, UK, as a remote access and device management solution for education. The company was acquired by Investcorp and rebranded to Ativion in June 2024. In 2020, Ativion acquired Netop, a Danish remote access software company, expanding its education technology portfolio.
Impero.summary_abuse: Impero (now Ativion) is an educational technology and remote access platform with no notable abuse profile. The software is used by schools and corporate IT teams for device monitoring and management. No documented incidents of the platform being exploited for malicious purposes.

Imperva.description: Imperva is a cyber security software and services company for networking, data, and application security.
Imperva.history: Imperva was founded in 2002 and went public in 2011. It is a major cybersecurity company providing WAF, DDoS mitigation, CDN, and data security products. Imperva was acquired by Thales Group (via Francisco Partners) in 2023.
Imperva.summary_abuse: Imperva is an enterprise cybersecurity and CDN provider focused on protecting against attacks rather than enabling them. Its WAF and DDoS mitigation products are widely deployed by financial institutions, e-commerce, and government organizations. No notable abuse signal is associated with this technology.

Importify.description: Importify is a dropshipping app that sources products from hundreds of suppliers, facilitating their integration into your online store for sale.
Importify.history: Importify is a Shopify dropshipping app developed by Importify Limited, enabling merchants to source and import products from AliExpress, Alibaba, Etsy, and other suppliers. The app operates within the Shopify ecosystem and targets small e-commerce entrepreneurs entering dropshipping.
Importify.summary_abuse: Importify is a dropshipping tool with elevated abuse potential relative to typical SaaS tools. Dropshipping platforms have been linked to counterfeit goods, trademark infringement, and deceptive shipping practices by bad-actor merchants. The tool itself does not actively facilitate fraud, but its ecosystem (AliExpress dropshipping) has a documented history of consumer deception involving fake products and long shipping delays misrepresented as domestic fulfilment.

Impresee.description: Impresee is a tool that enhances ecommerce by providing an actionable search bar to improve product discovery and user navigation.
Impresee.history: Impresee is an AI-powered e-commerce search and navigation platform founded in Chile, offering visual search (photo, sketch, hashtag), text search, and behavioral analytics to e-commerce merchants across Shopify, Magento, WooCommerce, and other platforms. The company operates independently and serves merchants across Latin America and globally. No notable acquisitions or rebranding events are documented.
Impresee.summary_abuse: Impresee is a backend search bar and navigation plugin for e-commerce stores. It has no consumer-facing hosting surface and no documented abuse signal.

ImpressCMS.description: ImpressCMS is an open-source content management system (CMS) designed for creating and managing dynamic websites.
ImpressCMS.history: ImpressCMS is an open-source PHP CMS that forked from XOOPS in 2008 after differences within the XOOPS development community. It has been maintained by a small volunteer community since then.
ImpressCMS.summary_abuse: ImpressCMS is a self-hosted open source CMS with no notable abuse signal at the platform level. Outdated installations may be vulnerable to exploits, but this is typical for any legacy PHP CMS.

ImpressPages.description: ImpressPages is an open-source content management system (CMS) that enables users to create and manage websites easily, featuring a user-friendly interface and customizable templates.
ImpressPages.history: ImpressPages was created in 2009 by three Lithuanian developers as an open-source PHP CMS. It won the Packt Most Promising Open-Source Project Award in 2011 and raised EUR 200,000. The project was discontinued after version 5.0.3 in 2018 due to lack of resources.
ImpressPages.summary_abuse: ImpressPages is an abandoned open-source CMS with no notable abuse profile. As a discontinued self-hosted PHP CMS, older installations may carry unpatched vulnerabilities, but the project is not associated with abuse campaigns.

Impressure.description: Impressure is a platform for creating and publishing offer paths, dynamic forms, and surveys through a user-friendly interface.
Impressure.history: Impressure is a small startup that provides landing page and offer path creation tools, targeting performance marketers and lead generation campaigns. The platform allows users to host surveys and forms on provided domains or custom domains.
Impressure.summary_abuse: Impressure presents moderate abuse potential due to its offer path and landing page features with custom domain support and a free trial. This combination is commonly exploited for phishing landing pages and deceptive lead generation. The platform serves performance marketers, and while legitimate uses are prevalent, the tooling characteristics match patterns seen in scam funnel operations. No specific documented incidents were found.

Imunify360.description: Imunify360 is a comprehensive security platform for Linux web servers which utilises machine learning technology and other advanced techniques to provide protection against various types of cyber threats, such as malware, viruses, and other attacks.
Imunify360.history: Imunify360 is a security platform for Linux web servers developed by CloudLinux, a company focused on Linux-based shared hosting environments. CloudLinux has been in business since 2010 and Imunify360 was launched as a security product line.
Imunify360.summary_abuse: Imunify360 is a security tool designed to protect web servers from malware and attacks. It is a defensive product with no consumer-facing attack surface and no notable abuse signal.

Imweb.description: Imweb is a ecommerce website builder software.
Imweb.history: Imweb (아임웹) was founded in 2010 in Seoul, South Korea, as an e-commerce website builder targeting Korean small businesses and entrepreneurs. It has grown to become one of Korea's popular website and online store building platforms, similar to Wix or Squarespace in functionality.
Imweb.summary_abuse: Imweb is a Korean e-commerce website builder with no notable abuse profile. As a website builder platform serving authenticated Korean businesses, it has standard merchant vetting processes. No documented incidents of systematic abuse involving Imweb stores have been reported.

In Cart Upsell & Cross-Sell.description: In Cart Upsell & Cross-Sell is a Shopify app built by InCart Upsell, provides targeted upsells and cross-sells directly in your cart and product page.
In Cart Upsell & Cross-Sell.history: In Cart Upsell & Cross-Sell is a Shopify app developed by InCart Upsell, providing targeted upsell and cross-sell offers within the shopping cart to increase average order value for Shopify merchants.
In Cart Upsell & Cross-Sell.summary_abuse: In Cart Upsell & Cross-Sell is a Shopify cart optimization app with no notable abuse profile. It operates within Shopify's reviewed app ecosystem and provides standard e-commerce conversion features. No abuse incidents have been associated with this app.

Inblog.description: Inblog is a content management system designed for SEO, featuring CTAs and analytics for revenue optimization.
Inblog.history: Inblog was founded in 2023 and is headquartered in Seoul, South Korea. It is a SEO-focused blogging CMS built on Next.js and Supabase, targeting content marketing teams.
Inblog.summary_abuse: Inblog is a hosted CMS platform with a free tier. While users can publish content under custom domains, the platform targets legitimate content marketing use. No significant abuse patterns have been documented.

INBOX.description: INBOX is an email marketing system designed to create, manage, and track email campaigns for communication, promotions, or newsletters.
INBOX.history: Inbox.lv is the largest email service in Latvia, founded in 1998 and launching the Inbox.lv project in 2001. It expanded to Lithuania in 2007, added domain email services via inbox.eu in 2011, and the Estonian service mail.ee in 2014. Its servers are located in Latvia and comply with GDPR.
INBOX.summary_abuse: INBOX (Inbox.lv) is a legitimate Latvian email provider. Free-tier email services can be used for spam or phishing campaigns, but Inbox.lv is primarily a regional consumer email service rather than a bulk mailing platform. No major documented abuse incidents are associated with this specific provider.

Incapsula.description: Incapsula is a cloud-based application delivery platform. It uses a global content delivery network to provide web application security, DDoS mitigation, content caching, application delivery, load balancing and failover services.
Incapsula.history: Incapsula was founded in 2009 and acquired by Imperva in 2014. It is Imperva's cloud-based CDN, WAF, and DDoS mitigation platform. After Imperva's acquisition by Thales Group in 2023, Incapsula continues operating under the Imperva umbrella.
Incapsula.summary_abuse: Incapsula is an enterprise CDN and web application security platform from Imperva. It is designed to protect websites from attacks rather than enable them. No notable abuse signal is associated with this technology.

Incident.io.description: Incident.io is a Slack-integrated incident management tool used to announce, manage, and resolve all incidents in a single channel.
Incident.io.history: Incident.io was founded in 2021 in London by Chris Evans, Stephen Whitworth, and Pete Hamilton. It quickly gained traction in the DevOps and SRE community as a Slack-native incident management platform and has raised significant venture funding.
Incident.io.summary_abuse: Incident.io is a B2B incident management tool for engineering teams. It operates within enterprise Slack workspaces under contractual agreements and has no notable abuse signal.

Includable.history: Includable is a small development agency and consultancy based in the Netherlands, building web applications, APIs, and mobile apps for social enterprises and startups. No significant ownership or deprecation events documented.
Includable.summary_abuse: Includable is a small development agency with no notable abuse signal; it provides custom software development services with no consumer-facing hosted platform that could be exploited by bad actors.

Indeed.description: Indeed is a platform that provides embedded code for tracking job-related clicks and supports job search functionality.
Indeed.history: Indeed was founded in 2004 by Paul Forster and Rony Kahan in Austin, Texas. It became the world's largest job board. In 2012, Recruit Holdings of Japan acquired a majority stake, and Indeed became a wholly owned subsidiary of Recruit by 2013.
Indeed.summary_abuse: Indeed embeds tracking pixels on employer websites for job click attribution. The platform itself is legitimate but at massive scale, job boards attract fake job postings used for phishing, resume harvesting, and employment scams. Indeed maintains AUP policies and fraud detection, but job posting fraud is a persistent industry challenge on all major job boards.

Index Exchange.description: Index Exchange is a customizable exchange technology that enables sell side media firms to monetize ad inventories programmatically and in real time.
Index Exchange.history: Index Exchange was founded in 2003 in Canada, originally as Casale Media. It rebranded to Index Exchange in 2015 and became a leading independent programmatic ad exchange. It remains independently owned and operates as a supply-side platform connecting publishers with advertisers.
Index Exchange.summary_abuse: Index Exchange is a programmatic advertising exchange. SSPs and ad exchanges are core components of the ad-tech ecosystem that enable real-time bidding. They are frequently implicated in malvertising campaigns because ad creatives can be injected into publisher pages through the bidding process. While Index Exchange is a legitimate major exchange, the nature of programmatic advertising means malicious ads can propagate through the system despite AUP enforcement.

Indexhibit.history: Indexhibit is a minimalist CMS created around 2005 by Vaska for artists and designers to showcase their work. It was widely used in the creative community but has seen declining usage with the rise of modern portfolio platforms.
Indexhibit.summary_abuse: Indexhibit is a niche self-hosted CMS for creative portfolios with no notable abuse signal.

Indexic.description: Indexic is a booking management platform designed for tours, activities, and rental operations.
Indexic.history: Founded in 2010 and headquartered in Mount Pleasant, South Carolina, Indexic provides booking management software for tours, activities, and rental operations under brands including aReservation and aWaiver; no notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Indexic.summary_abuse: Indexic is a niche B2B booking platform for tour and rental operators with no notable abuse signal; it processes payments and manages reservations for legitimate businesses and has no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudsters.

Indi.description: Indi is a video social network where everyone - artists, brands, retailers, nonprofits, celebrities and individuals - can connect with fans and supporters to interact directly with your brand utilising exclusive Video Challenges and Video Threads tailor made by you.
Indi.history: Indi is a video social network platform connecting artists, brands, and celebrities with fans through video challenges and threads. No notable ownership changes documented.
Indi.summary_abuse: Indi is a niche video social platform. It has user-generated content features which carry inherent moderation challenges, but no notable abuse patterns targeting fraudsters or scammers have been documented.

Indico.description: Indico is an open-source event management system designed for organizing scientific conferences and workshops.
Indico.history: Indico was developed at CERN (Switzerland) and first released in 2002. It is an open-source event and conference management system widely used in scientific institutions, universities, and research organizations worldwide.
Indico.summary_abuse: Indico is an open-source conference management system developed by CERN and used by scientific institutions. It has no consumer-facing hosted service and no notable abuse signal.

Indy.history: Indy is an open-source web server and internet component library originally developed for Delphi and C++ Builder. The IndyProject has been maintained by the community for many years, though activity has slowed significantly.
Indy.summary_abuse: Indy (IndyProject) is an open-source web server and internet component library for Delphi. It is a legacy developer tool with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Inertia.js.description: Inertia.js is a protocol for creating monolithic single-page applications.
Inertia.js.history: Inertia.js was created by Jonathan Reinink and first released in 2019. It provides a protocol for building single-page applications using server-side routing and controllers, bridging the gap between traditional server-rendered apps and modern SPA frameworks. It gained significant popularity in the Laravel community.
Inertia.js.summary_abuse: Inertia.js is an open-source JavaScript SPA framework library with no abuse signal. It is a developer tool with no consumer-facing hosted service.

InEvent.description: InEvent is event management software designed to streamline planning, organization, and execution of virtual, hybrid, and in-person events.
InEvent.history: InEvent was founded in 2013 in Brazil and went through Y Combinator. The company provides enterprise event management software for in-person, hybrid, and virtual events. It serves Fortune 500 companies and has expanded its platform to cover the full event lifecycle from planning to post-event analytics.
InEvent.summary_abuse: InEvent is an enterprise event management platform with no notable abuse profile. The service targets corporate and enterprise customers requiring authenticated account setup and contractual agreements. There is no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited for phishing or spam operations.

InfernoJS.description: InfernoJS is a JavaScript library designed for building user interfaces, known for its high performance and similarity to React.js in terms of API and structure.
InfernoJS.history: InfernoJS was created by Dominic Gannaway (who later joined the React core team at Facebook) and released around 2016. It is a high-performance React-like library focused on minimal bundle size and maximum rendering speed.
InfernoJS.summary_abuse: InfernoJS is an open-source JavaScript UI library with no abuse signal. It is a developer tool with no consumer-facing hosted service.

Infinite Scroll.history: Infinite Scroll is a jQuery plugin created by Paul Irish and later maintained by David DeSandro. It implements automatic pagination and infinite scrolling for web pages. It has been available since approximately 2011.
Infinite Scroll.summary_abuse: Infinite Scroll is a JavaScript library for implementing infinite scroll pagination. It has no abuse signal and is a standard frontend UI pattern library.

Infinity.description: Infinity is an all-in-one workspace that allows project organization, task tracking, and team collaboration in a centralized platform.
Infinity.history: Infinity (startinfinity.com) was founded in 2018 as a customizable all-in-one workspace for project management and team collaboration. No notable ownership changes since launch.
Infinity.summary_abuse: Infinity is a project management SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend productivity tool with no consumer-facing surface that scammers would typically exploit.

Influence.description: Influence is a conversion optimisation tool for ecommerce that leverages real-time social proof.
Influence.history: Influence was founded in 2018 by Kirti Prakash as a social proof conversion tool for ecommerce websites, helping businesses display real-time customer activity notifications.
Influence.summary_abuse: Influence embeds social proof notification widgets on ecommerce sites. Its conversion-focused notifications could in principle be used to display fabricated activity counts on scam storefronts, though no specific abuse cases have been documented.

Influenster.description: Influenster is a product reviews widget that displays feedback and ratings from shoppers on websites.
Influenster.history: Influenster was founded in 2010 by Aydin Acar and Elizabeth Scherle in New York as a product discovery and reviews platform. It was acquired by Bazaarvoice in 2019, becoming a subsidiary of that Austin-based ratings and reviews company.
Influenster.summary_abuse: Influenster is a product reviews and discovery platform now owned by Bazaarvoice. It collects user-generated reviews and product feedback. The platform has no notable history of being used for fraud or scam hosting, though fake review injection is a general risk in any consumer reviews product.

Influx CMS.description: Influx CMS is a medical marketing platform specialising in custom web design and marketing for doctors, surgeons, and medical spas.
Influx CMS.history: Influx CMS (Influx Marketing) is a US-based medical marketing platform providing website and CMS solutions specifically for healthcare professionals including doctors, surgeons, and medical spas.
Influx CMS.summary_abuse: Influx CMS targets the medical marketing niche with no notable abuse signal. It serves healthcare providers and has no notable history of exploitation by bad actors.

Infogram.description: Infogram is a web-based data visualisation and infographics platform.
Infogram.history: Infogram was founded in February 2012 in Riga, Latvia by Uldis Leiterts, Raimonds Kaže, and Alise Dīrika as a web-based data visualization and infographics tool. In May 2017 it was acquired by Prezi, the presentation software company, becoming Prezi's first acquisition.
Infogram.summary_abuse: Infogram is a data visualization and infographics SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is used for legitimate chart and report creation with no consumer-facing surface that scammers typically target.

Infolinks.description: Infolinks is an online advertising platform for publishers and advertisers.
Infolinks.history: Infolinks was founded in 2007 by Oren Dobronsky and Yariv Davidovich in Palo Alto, California, with R&D in Tel Aviv, Israel. The company pioneered in-text advertising, inserting keyword-triggered ad units into website content. It was acquired in 2018 for less than the total equity raised. The company serves publishers through multiple ad formats including inText, inFrame, inSearch, and inTag.
Infolinks.summary_abuse: Infolinks is an in-text and display advertising network that injects JavaScript-based ad units into publisher websites. As an ad network, it processes large volumes of behavioral tracking data from visitors across hundreds of thousands of websites. The platform has been criticized for intrusive ad formats and slow payment by some publishers. In-text advertising networks are occasionally implicated in malvertising chains, though no large-scale Infolinks-specific malvertising incident has been publicly documented. The platform's trackers follow users across the web.

Infomaniak.description: Infomaniak is a hosting company based in Geneva, Switzerland.
Infomaniak.history: Founded in 1994 in Geneva, Switzerland, Infomaniak is one of Switzerland's leading independent web hosting and cloud service providers. Known for its privacy-focused approach and Swiss data sovereignty, it has grown organically without external investment.
Infomaniak.summary_abuse: Infomaniak is a Swiss web hosting provider with low abuse signal. As a legitimate European hosting company with strong privacy commitments and Swiss data protection laws, it maintains an AUP and responds to abuse reports. Shared hosting services carry some inherent risk of being used to host malicious content, but Infomaniak's Swiss legal environment provides stronger accountability than offshore alternatives.

INFOnline.history: INFOnline is a German online audience measurement and digital analytics provider focused on the German-speaking media market. It operates the agof (Arbeitsgemeinschaft Online-Forschung) measurement system used by German publishers.
INFOnline.summary_abuse: INFOnline is a German web analytics and audience measurement service for publishers. As a tracking pixel/analytics provider, it collects user data across publisher sites but operates under German/EU privacy law. No fraud or malware abuse patterns are documented.

Infor.description: Infor is a resource planning solution designed to streamline operations, manage workflows, and support data-driven decision-making across various business functions.
Infor.history: Infor was formed in 2002 through the merger of GEAC Computer Corporation and Intentia, consolidating various ERP software businesses. Koch Industries acquired a majority stake in 2017, and Infor merged fully into Koch in 2020. It remains one of the world's largest enterprise software companies focused on industry-specific cloud ERP solutions.
Infor.summary_abuse: Infor has no notable abuse signal. It is a large enterprise ERP and CRM platform used by Fortune 500 companies, with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters target. Its enterprise sales model and large corporate customer base effectively prevent the casual abuse seen in self-serve platforms.

Informa Markets.description: Informa Markets is a platform that facilitates trade, innovation, and growth across various industries and specialist markets.
Informa Markets.history: Informa Markets is the trade events and B2B exhibitions division of Informa plc, a FTSE 100 company founded in 1998 through the merger of IBC Group and LLP Group. The division operates over 450 B2B brands and 500 live events annually across specialist industries worldwide.
Informa Markets.summary_abuse: Informa Markets is a large B2B trade events platform under Informa plc with no notable abuse signal. It facilitates legitimate industry trade shows and events rather than consumer-facing hosting that scammers would exploit.

Informizely.description: Informizely is a customer feedback platform used to run surveys on websites and applications to understand user behavior and generate actionable insights.
Informizely.history: Informizely was founded in 2014 by Diderik Broos in Amsterdam, Netherlands. It was formerly known as Insitez. The company remains bootstrapped with no external funding. It offers targeted website surveys and feedback collection tools, serving customers including Bose, Domino's Pizza, EMC2, and Scout24.
Informizely.summary_abuse: Informizely is a website survey platform with no notable abuse signals. The product collects visitor survey responses on behalf of its customers and does not provide file hosting or URL redirection features that could be exploited. As a Netherlands-based company, it is subject to GDPR and EU data protection law. No documented abuse incidents.

InforUMobile.description: InforUMobile is a multi-channel system that enables digital communication with customers, offering various channels for interacting and engaging with users, developed by the Shamir Systems Group.
InforUMobile.history: InforUMobile (operating under Shamir Systems) is an Israeli multi-channel digital marketing platform headquartered in Rishon LeZion, Israel, active in the mobile marketing arena since at least 2001. The platform offers SMS, email, WhatsApp messaging, chatbots, landing pages, surveys, and digital catalogs primarily for the Israeli business market. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are publicly documented.
InforUMobile.summary_abuse: InforUMobile provides bulk SMS, email, and WhatsApp messaging services. Mass messaging platforms carry inherent spam risk: they can be used to send unsolicited marketing or phishing messages at scale. InforUMobile serves the Israeli market and operates under Israeli communication regulations. While no specific high-profile abuse incidents are documented, bulk SMS and email platforms generally require monitoring to prevent spam campaigns by customers.

Infoset.description: Infoset is an advanced communication and support solutions.
Infoset.history: Founded in 2020 in Istanbul, Turkey by Samed and Sercan Düzçay as an all-in-one omnichannel customer communication platform for businesses. The startup raised seed funding from French IT company IT Task Force. In March 2023, Infoset was acquired by Eliptik Yazilim ve Ticaret.
Infoset.summary_abuse: Infoset is a B2B customer support SaaS platform with no notable public abuse incidents. As a business-facing tool requiring account registration, its attack surface for abuse is limited. No documented cases of the platform being exploited for spam, phishing, or fraudulent activity.

Infront.description: Infront is a sports marketing company that specializes in the management, marketing, and distribution of sports media rights and events, providing services to sports federations, leagues, clubs, and media partners worldwide.
Infront.history: Infront Sports & Media was founded in October 2002 in Switzerland through a merger of sports marketing companies CWL and Prisma Sports & Media. The company was acquired by Dalian Wanda Group (China) in 2015, making it a subsidiary of the Wanda Sports Group.
Infront.summary_abuse: Infront is a sports rights management and media company with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a B2B service for sports federations and broadcasters rather than a consumer-facing platform.

InkSoft.description: InkSoft is a comprehensive platform offering online designer tools and ecommerce solutions tailored for printing companies.
InkSoft.history: InkSoft was founded around 2009 and is based in the United States. The platform provides e-commerce and web-to-print software for the decorated apparel and promotional products industry. The company partnered with alphabroder and integrates with Printavo for order management. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
InkSoft.summary_abuse: InkSoft is a niche B2B ecommerce and design platform for print shops, used by businesses to create customer-facing online stores. While it hosts customer storefronts, the platform is targeted at the print industry with verified business accounts. No significant abuse incidents or fraud reports have been documented.

InMoment.description: InMoment provides SaaS based customer survey and enterprise feedback management solutions.
InMoment.history: InMoment was founded in 2002 in Salt Lake City, Utah. It has grown through acquisitions including Wootric, ReviewTrackers, and Lexalytics to become a comprehensive enterprise customer experience (CX) intelligence platform.
InMoment.summary_abuse: InMoment is an enterprise customer experience and survey platform. It collects feedback data from customers under contractual enterprise agreements and has no notable abuse signal.

Innervate.description: Innervate is a company that provides a plug-and-play solution for dynamic customer experience orchestration across various formats and channels, leveraging existing systems, data, and teams to enhance and streamline customer interactions.
Innervate.history: Innervate was founded in 2014 as RevJet by Serge Ioffe, Mitchell Weisman, and Craig Z in San Carlos, California. The company focused on dynamic creative optimization for digital advertising. LendingTree acquired RevJet in December 2017. In May 2023, the company relaunched as Innervate and positioned itself as a dynamic content orchestration platform serving enterprise advertisers across display, video, social, and mobile channels.
Innervate.summary_abuse: Innervate is a dynamic creative optimization platform used by enterprise advertisers with no notable direct abuse signals. As an advertising technology platform, it processes audience and behavioral data to serve personalized ad creatives, raising standard privacy considerations. No documented abuse incidents or security research reports against this platform.

InnoShop.description: InnoShop is an open-source ecommerce platform providing flexible tools for building and managing online stores.
InnoShop.history: InnoShop is an open-source e-commerce platform built on Laravel, developed by the innocommerce team who describe it as drawing on 15 years of industry experience. It is actively maintained on GitHub and supports multi-language, multi-currency, and a plugin/theme architecture.
InnoShop.summary_abuse: InnoShop is a self-hosted open-source e-commerce framework. As with any open-source e-commerce platform, it could be deployed to run fake storefronts, but the tool itself has no specific abuse signal and the project maintains no central hosted infrastructure.

Innovid Advertising Measurement.description: Innovid Advertising Measurement is a solution for assessing and analyzing the performance of advertising campaigns across different platforms and devices.
Innovid Advertising Measurement.history: Innovid was founded in 2007 by Zvika Netter, Tal Chalozin, and Zack Zigdon as an independent CTV and video ad serving platform. It went public via SPAC merger with ION Acquisition Corp 2 in 2021 (NYSE: CTV). In November 2024, Mediaocean announced its acquisition of Innovid for approximately $500 million, taking it private.
Innovid Advertising Measurement.summary_abuse: Innovid is an enterprise ad serving and measurement platform for CTV advertising. As ad-tech infrastructure, it is adjacent to tracking and profiling but operates in the legitimate B2B advertising space. No notable fraud or scam abuse has been documented.

Innovorder.description: Innovorder is a point-of-sale solution for restaurateurs that uses AI to support order management and in-store operations.
Innovorder.history: Founded in 2014 in Paris, France by Jérôme Varnier, Romain Melloul, and Olivier Loverde, who met at HEC business school. In 2016 Innovorder raised €1 million and acquired La Caisse Tactile, a POS software startup. A Series A round of €10 million followed in July 2019 with participation from Bpifrance, Kreaxi, and FJ Labs. Total funding reached $16.4M. The platform serves major French food service chains including KFC, Quick, and Courtepaille.
Innovorder.summary_abuse: Innovorder is a restaurant POS and operations SaaS platform used by verified food service businesses. The platform processes payment and order data in controlled B2B environments. No significant abuse incidents have been documented, and the platform has limited consumer-facing exposure that could enable fraud.

Inplayer.description: Inplayer is a platform that enables video streaming at scale, supports audience expansion across devices, and provides tools for content monetization.
Inplayer.history: InPlayer was founded in 2010 (originally as Invideous Limited) in Macedonia as a video paywall and monetization platform. It was acquired by JW Player to expand its monetization and audience insights offering.
Inplayer.summary_abuse: InPlayer is a video paywall and streaming monetization platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves media companies and content owners with authenticated streaming, which limits its exposure to content abuse.

Inputflow.description: Inputflow is a tool for creating multi-step forms in Webflow with full customization options.
Inputflow.history: Inputflow is a small SaaS tool for creating multi-step forms in Webflow without coding. No notable history or ownership events documented.
Inputflow.summary_abuse: Inputflow is a Webflow form-builder tool with no notable abuse signal. It serves designers building forms in Webflow and has no consumer-facing surface targeted by bad actors.

inSales.description: inSales is a SaaS ecommerce platform with multichannel integration.
inSales.history: inSales is a Russian SaaS e-commerce platform with multichannel integration capabilities. It has served the Russian e-commerce market since the late 2000s.
inSales.summary_abuse: inSales is a Russian e-commerce SaaS platform with no documented notable abuse cases.

inSided.description: inSided is the only Customer Success Community Platform built to help SaaS companies improve customer success and retention.
inSided.history: inSided was founded in 2012 in Amsterdam as a customer success community platform for SaaS companies. In January 2022, Gainsight acquired inSided to integrate community capabilities into its customer success suite.
inSided.summary_abuse: inSided is a B2B community platform for SaaS companies with no documented abuse cases.

Insider.description: Insider is the first integrated Growth Management Platform helping digital marketers drive growth across the funnel, from Acquisition to Activation, Retention, and Revenue from a unified platform powered by Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning.
Insider.history: Founded in 2012 in Istanbul, Turkey by Hande Çilingir and co-founders. Originally a marketing automation platform, Insider expanded globally and relocated its headquarters to Singapore. The company achieved unicorn status in March 2022 with a $1.22 billion valuation after a $121 million Series D from QIA. In 2024, it raised $500 million in a Series E led by General Atlantic, reaching a $2 billion valuation. In late 2025 the company rebranded as Insider One.
Insider.summary_abuse: Insider is a large-scale B2B customer data and marketing automation platform used by enterprises. As a martech tool processing visitor behavioral data at scale, it collects browsing and engagement data across client websites, raising standard privacy considerations. The platform is subject to GDPR and has compliance controls. No documented incidents of platform misuse for fraud or phishing have been reported.

Insightly CRM.description: Insightly CRM is a cloud-based customer relationship management software, helps businesses manage leads, contacts, and opportunities, track customer interactions, create custom reports, automate workflows, and integrate with third-party applications, improving customer engagement and streamlining business processes.
Insightly CRM.history: Founded in 2009 in Perth, Australia by Anthony Smith as a CRM integrated with Google Apps. In 2012 the company relocated to San Francisco and raised $3 million from Emergence Capital. Insightly grew into a CRM and project management tool for SMBs with a freemium model. In July 2024, private equity firm Crest Rock Partners acquired Insightly and merged it with Unbounce, a landing-page optimization platform.
Insightly CRM.summary_abuse: Insightly CRM is a cloud CRM platform for small and medium-sized businesses. As a B2B tool requiring account registration, it has limited exposure to external abuse. No documented incidents of the platform being used for phishing, fraud, or other malicious purposes have been reported.

Insignal.description: Insignal is a cloud-based application that tracks website traffic, heatmap, timeline, feedback, session recordings, and survey solutions.
Insignal.history: Insignal is a cloud-based web analytics and session recording tool tracking website traffic, heatmaps, feedback, and surveys. No notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Insignal.summary_abuse: Insignal is a web analytics and session recording SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It collects site visitor data for website owners, operating similarly to other analytics tools with standard telemetry collection.

Inso.description: Inso is an application for kindergartens and nurseries that facilitates communication with parents and supports the automated preparation of billing information.
Inso.history: Inso is a Polish SaaS application for kindergartens and nurseries, facilitating parent communication and automated billing. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Inso.summary_abuse: Inso is a closed B2B SaaS for childcare administration with no documented abuse signal. Its audience is preschools and nurseries.

Inspectlet.history: Inspectlet is a session recording and heatmap analytics tool launched around 2011, based in the United States. The company has remained independent and bootstrapped. In 2026 the platform added AI-powered session analysis capabilities. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Inspectlet.summary_abuse: Inspectlet records full visitor sessions including mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes on client websites. This makes it a high-sensitivity tool from a privacy perspective, as keystroke logging can inadvertently capture form inputs including passwords. The company provides GDPR guidance and masking options, but the session recording category generally carries elevated risk of capturing PII. No documented cases of the platform being actively abused for fraud, though operators are responsible for proper configuration.
