Instabot.description: Instabot is a conversion chatbot that understands your users, and curates information, answers questions, captures contacts, and books meetings instantly.
Instabot.history: Instabot was founded around 2016 in New York, United States. The platform provides AI chatbot software for lead generation and customer engagement on websites. The company offers white-label solutions for marketing agencies. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Instabot.summary_abuse: Instabot is a chatbot platform used for website lead capture and customer engagement. As a chat interface on business websites, it collects visitor contact information. The platform's white-label and agency features mean it can be deployed across many websites. No documented incidents of the platform being used for phishing or fraud have been reported, though chatbot platforms generally require AUP enforcement.

Instafeed.description: Instafeed is an official Instagram app.
Instafeed.history: Instafeed is a Shopify app that embeds Instagram feeds on Shopify storefronts. It is an official Instagram-connected app distributed via the Shopify App Store.
Instafeed.summary_abuse: Instafeed is a Shopify app for embedding Instagram content. It has no notable abuse signal and is distributed through Shopify's reviewed app marketplace.

Instafeed.js.description: A way to add Instagram photos to your website.
Instafeed.js.history: Instafeed.js was originally written in 2012 by Steven Schobert as a simple vanilla JavaScript library to display Instagram photos on websites. It reached version 2.1.0. Instagram API changes by Meta have complicated token management, requiring server-side components that the library cannot provide on its own.
Instafeed.js.summary_abuse: Instafeed.js has no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend JavaScript library for displaying Instagram photos with no server-side or hosting component that fraudsters would target.

Instagram Feed for WordPress.description: Display Instagram photos from any non-private Instagram accounts.
Instagram Feed for WordPress.history: Instagram Feed for WordPress (Smash Balloon Social Photo Feed) was created by Smash Balloon in 2013. It has grown to over 1.75 million active users and is one of the most widely used social media feed plugins for WordPress.
Instagram Feed for WordPress.summary_abuse: This WordPress plugin embeds Instagram feeds on websites. It fetches public Instagram data via the official API and has no notable abuse signal. As a widely used WordPress plugin it could theoretically appear on scam sites, but the plugin itself is not an abuse tool.

Instamojo.description: Instamojo is a Bangalore-based company that provides a platform for selling digital goods and collecting payment online.
Instamojo.history: Instamojo was founded in 2012 in Bangalore, India by Sampad Swain, Akash Gehani, and Aditya Sengupta. It grew to serve 2 million+ Indian MSMEs and provides payment links, payment gateway, and online store tools. The company is VC-backed and has remained one of the leading payment gateways for Indian small businesses.
Instamojo.summary_abuse: Instamojo has a minor abuse risk inherent to payment processing platforms. Payment gateways can be used to process fraudulent transactions or operate scam storefronts. Instamojo is PCI-DSS certified and targeted at legitimate Indian MSMEs. No major documented fraud incidents specific to the platform are on record.

Instana.description: Instana is a Kubernetes-native APM tool which is built for new-stack including Microservices and lately Serverless but also supports the existing VM based stacks  including several supported technologies.
Instana.history: Instana was founded in 2015 by Mirko Novakovic, Pete Abrams, and Fabian Lange in Germany, with offices in Chicago. The company specialized in automated APM for cloud-native and microservices applications, raising $57 million across multiple funding rounds including a Series C in 2018. IBM acquired Instana in November 2020 to strengthen its hybrid cloud observability portfolio. Post-acquisition, it became IBM Instana Observability.
Instana.summary_abuse: IBM Instana is an enterprise APM and observability platform with no abuse signals. It is deployed within enterprise cloud infrastructure environments for application performance monitoring and has no consumer-facing surface. IBM maintains comprehensive security and compliance programs for all its products.

Instant.description: Instant is a no-code, visual page builder for Shopify, allowing users to create custom landing pages and sections through a drag-and-drop interface without requiring any coding skills.
Instant.history: Instant is a no-code visual page builder for Shopify, enabling users to create landing pages through drag-and-drop without coding. No notable history events documented.
Instant.summary_abuse: Instant is a Shopify page builder that lets merchants create custom landing pages. While any page builder could be used on a fraudulent storefront, no specific abuse incidents have been documented for this tool.

Instant.Page.description: Instant.Page is a JavaScript library which uses just-in-time preloading technique to make websites faster.
Instant.Page.history: Created by Alexandre Dieulot around 2018, Instant.Page is a tiny MIT-licensed JavaScript snippet that prefetches pages when users hover over links, reducing perceived load times. It is independently maintained with no commercial backing.
Instant.Page.summary_abuse: Instant.Page is a small open-source prefetching library with no hosted service or consumer surface. It has no notable abuse signal.

InstantClick.description: InstantClick is a JavaScript library that speeds up your website, making navigation faster.
InstantClick.history: InstantClick was created by Alexandre Dieulot and first released in 2014. It works by prefetching links on hover, similar to Instant.Page which Dieulot later created. The library is MIT-licensed and available on GitHub but has not seen active development in several years, with the maintainer focusing on Instant.Page instead.
InstantClick.summary_abuse: InstantClick is a small open-source prefetching library with no hosted service and no consumer-facing surface. It has no notable abuse signal.

InstantCMS.history: InstantCMS was developed in Russia as an open-source CMS focused on social networking portals and dating sites. Built with PHP and MySQL, it uses MVC architecture with Bootstrap. The project has been maintained on GitHub under the instantsoft organization. Originally created circa 2008, it has remained a niche platform primarily used by Russian-speaking communities.
InstantCMS.summary_abuse: InstantCMS is a self-hosted open-source CMS with a niche user base, primarily used for social portals and dating sites. Dating and social networking sites built on the platform may attract spam, fake profiles, or scam activity. The platform itself has no documented anti-abuse program, and the small developer team means security response may be slow.

InstantGeo.description: InstantGeo is a service that provides IP geolocation to web pages
InstantGeo.history: InstantGeo is a small IP geolocation service providing location data to web pages. No notable ownership history documented.
InstantGeo.summary_abuse: InstantGeo provides IP geolocation data for web pages. IP geolocation services can be used by scammers to serve geo-targeted phishing pages, but this service is very small with no documented abuse incidents.

Instapage.description: Instapage is a cloud-based landing page platform designed for marketing teams and agencies.
Instapage.history: Founded in 2012 in San Francisco, California by Tyson Quick after he identified low landing page conversion rates as a key marketing problem. The company raised $2 million in seed funding followed by a $15 million Series A from Morgan Stanley Expansion Capital in 2018, reaching $34.6 million in total funding. Instapage served over 16,000 customers and reached $19.2 million in revenue by 2017.
Instapage.summary_abuse: Landing page builders like Instapage are regularly used in phishing campaigns because they allow anyone to create convincing branded pages under custom domains with minimal technical skill. Instapage has an AUP and takes down fraudulent pages, but the ease of creating realistic lookalike pages makes it an occasional choice for credential-harvesting attacks. The platform is overwhelmingly used for legitimate marketing purposes, but its abuse for phishing is a documented industry-wide issue for landing page tools.

Instatus.description: Instatus is a status and incident communication tool.
Instatus.history: Instatus is an indie developer-built status page service launched around 2020. The platform was created by a solo developer and remains independently operated. It serves SaaS companies such as cloudconvert, Linear, Podium, and Restream. No acquisitions or major ownership changes have occurred.
Instatus.summary_abuse: Instatus provides hosted status pages for software services. The platform's outputs are typically public informational pages about service uptime. No documented cases of Instatus being abused for phishing or fraud have been reported. Its simple status-page purpose provides minimal attack surface for misuse.

InSyncai.description: InSyncai offers a conversational platform for enterprises to design and build chatbots having applications in customer support and services.
InSyncai.history: InSyncai was founded around 2015 (formerly known as ShopInSync) by Raj Ramaswamy and Ashish Parnami in San Jose, California, as an AI-driven conversational platform for enterprise customer support and e-commerce.
InSyncai.summary_abuse: InSyncai is an enterprise chatbot platform with no notable abuse signal. It targets mid-market and enterprise customers with sales-gated pricing, making it an unlikely target for abuse by bad actors.

Intaker.description: Intaker is a service that enables law firms to send and receive text messages with clients, including automated follow-up messages.
Intaker.history: Intaker is a US-based legal tech SaaS platform providing AI-powered chat, lead capture, and intake automation specifically for law firms. The company offers pre-built chat scripts for over 1,400 legal case types and integrates with major legal software like Filevine. Founded in the late 2010s, it targets personal injury, family law, and other small-to-medium law firms in the US.
Intaker.summary_abuse: Intaker is a legal intake automation tool with no notable abuse signals. The platform is purpose-built for US law firms and processes potential client contact information. Given its focus on law firm intake, it is unlikely to be exploited for spam or phishing, as clients are actively seeking legal services.

Integral Ad Science.description: Integral Ad Science is an American publicly owned technology company that analyses the value of digital advertising placements.
Integral Ad Science.history: Founded in 2009 as AdSafe Media by six co-founders including Will Luttrell, rebranded to Integral Ad Science in 2012. Received major investment from Vista Equity Partners in 2014. IPO on NASDAQ in June 2021 under ticker IAS. In November 2025, NovaCap completed a $1.9B acquisition, taking the company private.
Integral Ad Science.summary_abuse: Integral Ad Science is an ad verification and brand safety platform deeply embedded in the digital advertising ecosystem. As ad-tech infrastructure, it processes massive amounts of audience data and web browsing behaviour, raising privacy concerns. It is used by major advertisers to verify ad viewability and brand safety, and while not directly abused, the underlying tracking infrastructure can be repurposed by bad actors in the broader ad ecosystem.

Integrately.description: Integrately is a click automation software that enables connection of multiple applications.
Integrately.history: Integrately was founded in 2020 by Karan Thakkar in Nashik, India. The platform provides workflow automation by connecting over 1,000 apps with ready-made automation templates, positioning as a user-friendly alternative to Zapier. The company has remained independent and bootstrapped. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Integrately.summary_abuse: Integrately is a workflow automation platform that connects business apps. Like similar automation tools, it can be misused if legitimate accounts are compromised to send spam or exfiltrate data via connected app integrations. The platform requires account authentication and has an AUP. No specific documented incidents of widespread abuse have been reported.

Intel Active Management Technology.description: Intel Active Management Technology (AMT) is a proprietary remote management and control system for personal computers with Intel CPUs.
Intel Active Management Technology.history: Intel AMT is hardware and firmware for out-of-band remote management of Intel vPro business computers, introduced around 2006 with Intel Core 2 Duo vPro platforms. In 2017, a critical remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2017-5689) was discovered affecting AMT from 2008 to 2017 Kaby Lake. The FSF has criticised AMT as a backdoor-like feature running outside OS control. Intel has continued to update AMT with each processor generation.
Intel Active Management Technology.summary_abuse: Intel AMT provides hardware-level remote access to enterprise PCs, running outside the OS on Intel's Management Engine. A 2017 critical vulnerability allowed unauthenticated remote code execution on millions of business machines. Nation-state actors and sophisticated threat groups have exploited AMT for persistence because AV tools and OS-level defenses cannot detect or block ME-resident code. The FSF has called AMT a potential backdoor.

Intelligems.description: Intelligems is a tool that facilitates profit optimization for ecommerce businesses by allowing easy testing of prices, discounts, and shipping rates with the aim of maximizing margins.
Intelligems.history: Intelligems was founded around 2020 as a Shopify-focused A/B testing and price optimization SaaS. It targets DTC ecommerce brands and has built a significant presence on the Shopify App Store. No major ownership changes or notable controversies documented.
Intelligems.summary_abuse: Intelligems is a Shopify A/B testing platform with no significant abuse signal. It processes ecommerce pricing and conversion data for legitimate brands and has no documented misuse patterns.

Intellimize.description: Intellimize is a platform that utilizes machine learning to optimize website experiences and increase conversions in real-time.
Intellimize.history: Founded in 2016 in San Mateo, California by Guy Yalif, Brian Webb, and Jin Lim, veterans from Yahoo, Twitter, and BrightRoll. Intellimize raised $52 million total across four funding rounds, including a $30 million round. The company provided AI-driven website personalization and optimization. It was acquired by Webflow and rebranded as Webflow Optimize.
Intellimize.summary_abuse: Intellimize is a B2B website optimization platform used by enterprise marketing teams. Now part of Webflow, it processes visitor behavioral data for personalization purposes. No documented abuse incidents have been reported. The platform has minimal consumer-facing exposure that could be exploited for fraud.

IntenseDebate.description: IntenseDebate is a blog commenting system that supports Typepad, Blogger and Wordpress blogs. The system allows blog owners to track and moderate comments from one place with features like threading, comment analytics, user reputation, and comment aggregation.
IntenseDebate.history: IntenseDebate launched in late 2007 as an enhanced blog commenting platform. Automattic, the company behind WordPress, acquired it in September 2008. It continued operating as an independent brand under Automattic but has received little development investment since and is largely dormant.
IntenseDebate.summary_abuse: IntenseDebate is a hosted comment system owned by Automattic. Comment platforms can attract spam and abusive content, but IntenseDebate is largely dormant with a small user base, limiting its exposure. No specific documented abuse incidents.

Interact.description: Interact is a tool for creating online quizzes.
Interact.history: Interact (tryinteract.com) was founded in 2013 as a quiz-building platform focused on lead generation. It has generated over 100 million leads through its platform. The company is independently run, with no known acquisitions or major ownership changes.
Interact.summary_abuse: Interact is a quiz marketing platform used for lead generation. Quiz platforms can be used for phishing-style social engineering if quiz results pages contain malicious links or fake prize claims, but no documented patterns of abuse specific to Interact are known.

InteractiveCalculator.description: InteractiveCalculator is a platform that enables users to create customized calculators that can be embedded on their website.
InteractiveCalculator.history: InteractiveCalculator is a SaaS platform enabling users to create embeddable custom calculators for their websites. No notable ownership history documented.
InteractiveCalculator.summary_abuse: InteractiveCalculator is an embeddable calculator widget platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a utility tool for websites and has no consumer surface that fraudsters typically exploit.

Interago.description: Interago is a Brazilian platform that aggregates modern digital tools to support and streamline online business operations.
Interago.history: Interago is a Brazilian digital platform developed by Eco Webdesign, offering website, e-commerce, and news portal creation tools for the Brazilian SMB market. The platform bundles CMS, CRM, email/WhatsApp marketing, and analytics. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Interago.summary_abuse: Interago is a niche Brazilian website platform with no notable abuse signal and no reported misuse patterns.

Interakt.description: Interakt is a messaging platform tailored for business communication, offering features such as secure messaging, file sharing, and customer support functionalities.
Interakt.history: Interakt was launched in October 2021 by Jio Haptik Technologies, a subsidiary of Reliance Industries. Haptik itself was founded in 2013 and acquired by Reliance Jio in 2019 for approximately $100 million. Interakt was created as a WhatsApp Business API platform targeting Indian SMEs and D2C brands. It has grown to serve over 5,000 businesses and integrates directly with the official WhatsApp Business Platform.
Interakt.summary_abuse: Interakt is a WhatsApp Business platform with limited abuse risk. As an official WhatsApp Business Solution Provider, it operates under Meta's strict API usage policies. The platform primarily targets Indian SMEs for legitimate business communications. No documented abuse incidents specific to Interakt. Bulk WhatsApp messaging platforms in general carry some risk of spam, but Interakt's API-based approach and BSP status means it adheres to Meta's anti-spam enforcement.

Intercom.description: Intercom is an American software company that produces a messaging platform which allows businesses to communicate with prospective and existing customers within their app, on their website, through social media, or via email.
Intercom.history: Intercom was founded in October 2011 in San Francisco by four Irish entrepreneurs: Eoghan McCabe, Des Traynor, Ciaran Lee, and David Barrett, who previously ran design consultancy Contrast. The company pioneered in-app customer messaging and has grown into a comprehensive AI-first customer service platform. Intercom raised $291.9 million in total VC funding and became a unicorn in 2018 at a $1.28 billion valuation. Revenue reached $283 million in 2024. In March 2026, the company raised $250 million in venture debt from Hercules Capital.
Intercom.summary_abuse: Intercom is a widely-used customer messaging platform with a low but non-zero abuse profile. The JavaScript widget embedded on customer websites can be spoofed in phishing campaigns, and Intercom's chat infrastructure has occasionally been used to send unsolicited messages. The company maintains a responsible disclosure program and actively enforces its terms of service. Overall, legitimate use dominates overwhelmingly and Intercom has strong security certifications.

Intercom Articles.description: Intercom Articles is a tool to create, organise and publish help articles.
Intercom Articles.history: Intercom was founded in Dublin in 2011 by Eoghan McCabe, Des Traynor, Ciarán Lee, and David Barrett. It raised over $500M in VC funding, including $125M Series D from Kleiner Perkins in 2018 and $250M in debt from Hercules Capital in March 2026. The company rebranded its core product as Fin in May 2026 while retaining Intercom as the parent entity. Articles is the knowledge base and help center module.
Intercom Articles.summary_abuse: Intercom Articles is part of the Intercom customer messaging platform. The platform has no notable abuse signal. Intercom's infrastructure could theoretically be used for phishing emails impersonating brands using its messaging features, but the company enforces usage policies.

Internet Brands.description: Internet Brands is a technology company that operates a variety of web portals and online communities, providing information and services to consumers and businesses across a range of industries.
Internet Brands.history: Founded in 1998 as CarsDirect.com out of Idealab, it became Internet Brands in 2005 and IPO'd on NASDAQ in 2007. Acquired by Hellman & Friedman for $640M in 2010 and then by KKR for $1.1B in 2014. Made a major acquisition of WebMD and Medscape for $2.8B in 2017. In 2022 recapitalized at a $12B+ valuation with KKR, Temasek and Warburg Pincus.
Internet Brands.summary_abuse: Internet Brands operates a large portfolio of vertical media websites, including WebMD. Some of its forums and community sites have been implicated in spam or low-quality content issues. As a large media network, some properties may have lax content moderation. No specific systemic abuse incidents are well-documented.

InterRed.description: InterRed is a software platform that provides integrated, future-proof publishing solutions for digital and print media management.
InterRed.history: InterRed GmbH is a German CMS and editorial system vendor headquartered in Siegen, Germany, serving primarily German-language print publishers and media companies. Its clients include Axel Springer, CHIP Online, and ZEIT Sprachen. Axel Springer holds a 24.9% minority stake. The company has remained focused on the German publishing market.
InterRed.summary_abuse: InterRed is a B2B editorial CMS for professional publishers with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Intersection Observer.description: Intersection Observer is a browser API that provides a way to observe the visibility and position of a DOM element relative to the containing root element or viewport.
Intersection Observer.history: The Intersection Observer API was proposed by Google engineers and first implemented in Chrome in 2016. It became a W3C candidate recommendation maintained by the Web Applications Working Group. Broad browser support was achieved by March 2019. It replaced expensive polling-based visibility detection approaches.
Intersection Observer.summary_abuse: Intersection Observer is a browser API standard with no hosted service and no abuse signal.

Intershop.description: Intershop is an ecommerce platform, tailored to the needs of complex business processes and major organisations.
Intershop.history: Founded in 1992 in Jena, Germany as NetConsult by Stephan Schambach, Karsten Schneider, and Wilfried Beeck. Renamed Intershop and created the first German online store in 1995. During the dot-com bubble the company reached an $11B valuation, then crashed to penny-stock levels. It has survived as a B2B e-commerce platform vendor listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, serving enterprise clients including HP, BMW, and Deutsche Telekom.
Intershop.summary_abuse: Intershop is an enterprise B2B e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used by large corporations and is not associated with consumer-facing fraud or scam patterns.

Intervo.description: Intervo is a platform for building and managing enterprise-grade voice agents at scale, enabling reliable voice automation, system integration, and deployment across large organizations.
Intervo.history: Intervo is an AI voice agent platform for building and managing enterprise-grade voice automation. It is both open-source and hosted, targeting large-scale enterprise deployments.
Intervo.summary_abuse: Intervo is a voice automation platform. AI voice agents could theoretically be used in voice phishing (vishing) scams, but no specific abuse incidents have been documented for this service. Enterprise-focused deployment reduces consumer-facing risk.

Intiaro.description: Intiaro is a provider of home decor and furniture products designed to support the creation of stylish living spaces.
Intiaro.history: Intiaro provides 3D product configuration, visualisation, and AR technology for the furniture and home furnishings industry. Headquartered in Greensboro, NC, with technology teams in Poland. Launched Configurator 3.0 in October 2025. No major ownership changes documented.
Intiaro.summary_abuse: Intiaro is a B2B 3D product visualization platform for furniture brands with no notable abuse signal.

Intice.description: Intice is a lead conversion tool designed for the automotive industry, streamlining customer engagement and improving the conversion process from inquiries to sales.
Intice.history: Founded in 2011, intice is an automotive-focused lead conversion and incentive marketing technology company based in Wesley Chapel, Florida. The company offers patented lead capture overlays and digital retailing tools for car dealerships, including integrations with Honda Digital Solutions Program. No major ownership changes documented.
Intice.summary_abuse: Intice is a niche B2B SaaS for automotive dealerships with no notable abuse signal. Lead capture overlays can be aggressive but no documented dark patterns or fraud patterns specific to Intice are known.

Invenio.description: Invenio is an open-source software framework for large-scale digital repositories that provides the tools for management of digital assets in an institutional repository and research data management systems.
Invenio.history: Invenio was created at CERN and is used to power Zenodo, CERN's open-access research repository. It is an MIT-licensed Python framework for building large-scale digital repositories and research data management systems. Used at SLAC, Fermilab, EPFL, and other institutions. In 2019, institutions joined to develop InvenioRDM as an institution-agnostic distribution.
Invenio.summary_abuse: Invenio is a CERN-developed open-source digital repository framework used in academic and research institutions. It has no consumer surface and no known abuse signal.

Inventrue.description: Inventrue creates websites for RV, Motorsports and Trailer Dealerships.
Inventrue.history: Inventrue is a website platform for RV, motorsports, and trailer dealerships. It provides dealer websites with inventory management and lead generation features. No major ownership changes documented.
Inventrue.summary_abuse: Inventrue is a niche dealership website platform for RV and motorsports sectors with no notable abuse signal.

Inveon.description: Inveon is a technology company that has been delivering ecommerce infrastructure software and mcommerce applications.
Inveon.history: Founded in Istanbul, Turkey in 2006, Inveon is an enterprise e-commerce platform powering B2B and B2C digital commerce for mid-to-large retail brands. The company powers over 500 enterprise projects across 15 countries and has consistently appeared in Deloitte Technology Fast 50 for Turkey from 2017 to 2021.
Inveon.summary_abuse: Inveon is an enterprise e-commerce infrastructure platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves legitimate mid-to-large retail brands in Turkey and internationally.

Inveterate.description: Inveterate is a loyalty platform designed to support flexible, scalable loyalty programs across various industries.
Inveterate.history: Inveterate was founded around 2021 in Greenville, South Carolina, as a Shopify-native loyalty and membership platform. It targets DTC brands with paid membership and store credit programs as an alternative to traditional points-based loyalty schemes.
Inveterate.summary_abuse: Inveterate is a Shopify loyalty and membership SaaS with no notable abuse signal.

Invision Community.description: Invision Community is a scalable and customizable platform designed to build and grow online communities.
Invision Community.history: Invision Power Services (IPS) was founded in 2002 by Charles Warner and Matt Mecham after they left Jarvis Entertainment Group, which had owned Ikonboard. The software was originally called Invision Power Board (IPB). Version 4.0 was a full rewrite released in April 2015. The suite was later rebranded Invision Community and currently exists as both cloud-hosted and self-hosted options.
Invision Community.summary_abuse: Invision Community is forum and community software used by many legitimate websites. Forums hosted on the platform may attract spam bots or abusive users, but the platform itself provides moderation tools and has no systemic abuse pattern.

Invision Power Board.description: Invision Power Board is a commercial Internet forum software developed by Invision Community (formerly Invision Power Services).
Invision Power Board.history: Invision Power Board was the original name for the forum software created in 2002 by Invision Power Services (Charles Warner and Matt Mecham). The name was subsequently changed to Invision Community when the product was rebranded as a full community suite in 2015. IPB versions below 4.0 are deprecated; only the Invision Community brand continues under active development.
Invision Power Board.summary_abuse: Invision Power Board is the legacy name for Invision Community forum software. Older IPB installations may have unpatched vulnerabilities, creating abuse risks, but the product itself is legitimate community software with no systemic abuse pattern.

Invitario.description: Invitario is an event marketing platform that provides a digital invitation and guest management for business events.
Invitario.history: Invitario is a German event management and RSVP platform targeting mid-sized and enterprise companies. Managed by Christoph Hütter and Stefan Grossek, the platform emphasises GDPR compliance and supports live, hybrid, and virtual events. No known acquisitions or major ownership changes.
Invitario.summary_abuse: Invitario is a B2B corporate event management SaaS with no notable abuse signal.

Invoca.description: Invoca is an AI-powered call tracking and conversational analytics company.
Invoca.history: Invoca was founded in Santa Barbara, CA in 2008. It pioneered AI-powered call tracking and conversation intelligence, enabling marketers to measure offline phone conversions from digital campaigns. Raised $184M total including an $83M Series F in 2022 at a $1.1B valuation led by Silver Lake Waterman. Hit $103.8M in revenue in 2024.
Invoca.summary_abuse: Invoca is a call tracking and conversation intelligence platform used by enterprise marketers. Call tracking systems can be misused for caller ID spoofing but no specific incidents involving Invoca are documented. The company processes significant phone conversation data which raises privacy considerations.

Ionic.description: Ionic is an open-source framework that enables developers to create cross-platform mobile, web, and desktop applications using web technologies like HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Ionic.history: Ionic was created by Max Lynch, Ben Sperry, and Adam Bradley of Drifty Co. in 2013. v1 final released in May 2015; v4 in January 2019 adopted web components and framework-agnostic design. Ionic was acquired by OutSystems in November 2022 and continues to be actively developed as an open-source project.
Ionic.summary_abuse: Ionic is an open-source mobile app framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a well-established technology used by hundreds of thousands of developers for building legitimate cross-platform applications.

Ionicons.description: Ionicons is an open-source icon set crafted for web, iOS, Android, and desktop apps.
Ionicons.history: Ionicons is a free open-source icon library built and maintained by the Ionic team (now owned by OutSystems). It provides 1,300+ SVG icons with Material Design and iOS variants. Released alongside the Ionic framework circa 2013 under MIT license.
Ionicons.summary_abuse: Ionicons is a static open-source SVG icon library with no hosted service and no abuse signal.

IONOS.description: IONOS is the web hosting and cloud partner for small and medium-sized businesses.
IONOS.history: IONOS (formerly 1&1) was founded in 1988 in Germany. It operates as a subsidiary of United Internet AG. Originally known as 1&1 Internet, it rebranded to IONOS in 2018 to unify its European and US hosting brands. It is one of Europe's largest web hosting providers.
IONOS.summary_abuse: IONOS is a major European web hosting provider. Its large scale and broad customer base mean it hosts some malicious content, but IONOS maintains abuse reporting channels and cooperates with takedown requests. It has historically had a better abuse response reputation than some lower-cost providers. Its low entry pricing can attract some abuse.

ip-api.history: ip-api.com has been operating since 2012 as a free and commercial IP geolocation API. The service processes over 1 billion requests per day and is used by thousands of businesses worldwide. The company maintains servers in US, EU, and APAC regions. The founding organization and headquarters are not publicly disclosed.
ip-api.summary_abuse: ip-api.com is an IP geolocation API that is sometimes incorporated into malware, phishing kits, and fraud tools to geolocate victims and customize attacks based on their location. The free tier requires no API key or account registration, which makes it easy for bad actors to embed in malicious tools without attribution. While the vast majority of users are legitimate developers, ip-api is one of the more commonly found geolocation APIs in phishing kits and credential-stealing malware.

ip-label.history: ip-label is a French digital performance monitoring and web analytics company founded in 1997, providing network and application performance measurement for enterprise clients.
ip-label.summary_abuse: ip-label is an enterprise digital performance analytics platform with no notable abuse signal; it is used by large organizations to monitor their digital services.

IP2Location.io.description: IP2Location.io is a web service that provides geolocation data based on IP addresses through its API, allowing developers to integrate accurate physical location information into their applications.
IP2Location.io.history: IP2Location was founded around 2002 in Malaysia as a geolocation database provider. The IP2Location.io brand is the API-first service offering built on top of the long-standing IP2Location data products, targeting developers needing IP-based geolocation data via REST API.
IP2Location.io.summary_abuse: IP2Location.io is a geolocation API service with no notable abuse as a threat actor tool. Geolocation APIs can theoretically be used by fraud actors for footprinting, but IP2Location.io itself is a legitimate data API with no history of being weaponized. The service requires API keys and does not offer anonymous access.

iPaper.description: iPaper is a platform that converts printed materials into interactive digital catalogs designed to enhance customer engagement and support sales.
iPaper.history: iPaper was founded in 2006 in Aarhus, Denmark by Mark S. Rasmussen and Jesper Holm Joensen, and was formally spun off as its own company in 2008. The bootstrapped SaaS converts PDFs into interactive digital catalogs and has grown organically to serve over 1,200 brands globally.
iPaper.summary_abuse: iPaper is a B2B digital catalog SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is used by brands and retailers to host product catalogs, and does not expose consumer-facing free hosting that would attract phishing or scam operators.

ipapi.description: ipapi is a real-time geolocation and reverse IP lookup REST API.
ipapi.history: ipapi is an IP geolocation and reverse-lookup API service; no notable founding date, ownership changes, or events are publicly documented.
ipapi.summary_abuse: ipapi is an IP geolocation API with a free tier; like similar services it can be used by malicious actors to locate targets or customize phishing pages, but legitimate use dominates and no specific incidents are documented.

ipapi.co.description: ipapi.co is a web analytics provider with IP address lookup and location API.
ipapi.co.history: ipapi.co is developed and maintained by Kloudend, Inc., a US-based company. The service provides IP address lookup and geolocation with no signup required on the free tier, and is trusted by Fortune 500 companies.
ipapi.co.summary_abuse: ipapi.co is an IP geolocation API consumed primarily by backend systems. Like all IP lookup APIs it may be queried by malicious scripts for geolocation-based targeting, but this is a minor pattern and legitimate use dominates by a wide margin.

IPB.history: Invision Power Board (IPB) was created by Charles Warner and Matt Mecham around 2002 and became one of the leading commercial forum platforms of the 2000s. The product was rebranded as Invision Community in the 2010s to reflect expanded features beyond forums, while maintaining its commercial self-hosted and SaaS deployment options.
IPB.summary_abuse: Invision Community (IPB) is a legitimate forum and community software platform with no notable abuse profile. As forum software, it can occasionally host spam or objectionable content within user-run communities, but this is common to all forum platforms and not a defining characteristic. The vendor enforces terms of service.

ipbase.description: ipbase offers an API that supports both IPv4 and IPv6 and provides precise location data from IP addresses.
ipbase.history: ipbase.com is an IP geolocation API service built on Laravel by the everapihq team. It offers 150 free requests per month with paid plans starting at $9.99/month. No notable ownership changes documented.
ipbase.summary_abuse: ipbase.com is an IP geolocation API with no notable abuse signal. Like all IP lookup services it may be queried by automated scripts, but legitimate developer use dominates.

ipdata.description: ipdata is a JSON IP Address Geolocation API that allows to lookup the location of both IPv4 and IPv6.
ipdata.history: ipdata.co is an IP geolocation and threat intelligence API that has served over 20 billion requests. It operates across 11 AWS data centers globally and is used by clients including NASA and Disney. No notable ownership changes documented.
ipdata.summary_abuse: ipdata.co is an IP geolocation and threat intelligence API. Like all IP data services it may be queried by automated threat actors, but this is incidental to its primary legitimate use. The service itself includes threat intelligence features designed to help clients detect abuse.

IPFS.description: IPFS is a peer-to-peer hypermedia protocol that provides a distributed hypermedia web.
IPFS.history: IPFS (InterPlanetary File System) was designed by Juan Benet at Protocol Labs and released in 2014. It is a content-addressed, peer-to-peer file storage protocol. Protocol Labs has continued to develop the ecosystem including Filecoin for incentivized storage.
IPFS.summary_abuse: IPFS is heavily abused for hosting phishing pages, malware, and illegal content because its distributed architecture makes takedown extremely difficult. Once content is pinned and propagated, removal by any single party is impractical. Security researchers consistently document IPFS as a preferred hosting platform for phishing kits targeting financial institutions and cryptocurrency users. Malicious IPFS gateway URLs frequently bypass blocklists.

ipgeolocation.description: ipgeolocation is an IP Geolocation API and Accurate IP Lookup Database.
ipgeolocation.history: ipgeolocation.io was conceived in late 2017 and first released in May 2018 by JFreaks Software Solutions, based in Lahore, Pakistan. The service provides a free and paid IP geolocation API and database, offering data on IP location, ISP, timezone, currency, and security flags. By 2020 the service had reached 700 customers and approximately $290K in annual revenue.
ipgeolocation.summary_abuse: ipgeolocation.io is an IP geolocation API that, like similar services, can be incorporated into malware and phishing kits to geolocate victims and tailor attacks. The service offers a free tier with API keys and limited quota, which provides some attribution compared to no-key services. No specific documented abuse campaigns or security incidents are publicly attributed to ipgeolocation.io itself.

ipify.description: ipify is a service which provide public IP address API, IP geolocation API, VPN and Proxy detection API products.
ipify.history: ipify is a widely-used public IP address API providing a simple endpoint to return the caller's public IP; launched around 2014, it has served billions of requests and expanded into geolocation and proxy detection services.
ipify.summary_abuse: ipify is a public IP address and geolocation API with a free unauthenticated tier used in both legitimate applications and phishing kits that need to know a visitor's IP; legitimate use significantly outweighs abuse.

IPinfo.description: IPinfo is an IP address data provider.
IPinfo.history: IPinfo was founded around 2013 and is a trusted global leader in IP address data and intelligence. The company provides geolocation, ASN, VPN detection, carrier, and privacy detection APIs used by over 500,000 developers. The free IPinfo Lite tier provides unlimited requests for country-level and ASN data. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
IPinfo.summary_abuse: IPinfo is an IP geolocation and intelligence API. As a data enrichment service, it is widely used for legitimate fraud detection and security purposes. The data it provides about IP addresses including VPN detection and ASN information is itself used by security teams to combat abuse. No documented incidents of the platform being weaponized for malicious purposes have been reported.

IPInfoDB.description: IPInfoDB is the API that returns the location of an IP address.
IPInfoDB.history: IPInfoDB is a free IP geolocation database and API service, operating since around 2009. It provides downloadable geolocation databases and a free API key-based lookup service with no request limits. The service has been widely used by developers and network tools like Nmap for IP geolocation purposes. The company appears to be independently operated at small scale.
IPInfoDB.summary_abuse: IPInfoDB is an IP geolocation lookup service providing data about IP addresses. Like all geolocation services, this data can be used by legitimate security teams or misused for targeted attacks when combined with other data. The free API with no request limits has low barriers to access. No documented incidents of the platform being actively weaponized for malicious purposes have been reported.

iPresta.history: iPresta was founded on May 1, 2011 in Mashhad, Iran. The company is the official PrestaShop premium agency and partner in Iran, providing PrestaShop modules, themes, and development services with RTL (right-to-left) language support. The company has also expanded with a UAE office (iPresta UAE). iPresta is notably responsible for the PrestaShop RTL project on GitHub and has specialized in Iranian-specific payment integrations due to international payment restrictions in Iran.
iPresta.summary_abuse: iPresta is an Iranian PrestaShop agency providing ecommerce solutions in a market with restricted access to international payment systems. While the company itself is a legitimate PrestaShop partner, the Iranian context raises considerations around international payment alternatives and potential sanctions compliance issues for buyers outside Iran. No specific documented fraud or abuse incidents are associated with iPresta's modules or services.

ipstack.description: ipstack is a real-time IP to geolocation API capable of looking at location data and assessing security threats originating from risky IP addresses.
ipstack.history: ipstack is a real-time IP geolocation and security threat API provided by apilayer, a Vienna-based API marketplace. It is one of several APIs in the apilayer portfolio and has served millions of developer requests.
ipstack.summary_abuse: ipstack is a geolocation API that can be integrated into fraud toolkits for geographic targeting. Its free tier is accessible without KYC. Legitimate developer use dominates. No specific abuse incidents are documented.

iRaiser.description: iRaiser is a platform that provides charities with tailored solutions to optimize and manage fundraising activities.
iRaiser.history: iRaiser is a Dutch fundraising platform providing charities with tailored tools for managing and optimizing online donations. It serves nonprofits primarily in Europe.
iRaiser.summary_abuse: iRaiser is a nonprofit fundraising SaaS with no documented abuse cases. Its charity-focused model and modest size mean it presents minimal abuse risk.

Iress.description: Iress is a platform that provides software solutions for the financial services industry.
Iress.history: Founded in 1993 as Dunai Financial Systems by Peter Dunai, Neil Detering, and Hung Do in Australia. Acquired 80% by Bridge Information Systems in 1997. Listed on ASX in 2000 as BridgeDFS. Renamed Iress Market Technology in 2001, then Iress Limited in 2012. The company expanded globally through acquisitions. Used by 500,000+ financial services users.
Iress.summary_abuse: Iress is enterprise financial services software used by regulated institutions. It has no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

IrisLMS.description: IrisLMS comprehensive education management system, in order to support e-learning and provide suitable conditions for holding online and offline classes with all facilities.
IrisLMS.history: IrisLMS is an Iranian e-learning and education management system providing support for online and offline class management. The platform is hosted at irislms.ir targeting the Iranian education market. No significant public ownership or development history is documented.
IrisLMS.summary_abuse: IrisLMS is a niche Iranian LMS platform with no notable documented abuse signal.

Irroba.history: Irroba is one of Brazil's first e-commerce platforms, founded in 1998 in Franca, Brazil. It has been bootstrapped and grown to $5.6M annual revenue with a 67-person team by 2024, having activated over 21,000 stores. It integrates with major Brazilian marketplaces and payment providers.
Irroba.summary_abuse: Irroba is a Brazilian e-commerce platform with no specific documented abuse signal. Ecommerce platforms can host fraudulent stores, but no specific patterns of scam store hosting on Irroba are documented.

ISAY.description: ISAY (Internet Pages Management) is a CMS service provided by the Turkish Ministry of Interior for governorships, district governorships and various official websites.
ISAY.history: ISAY (Internet Sayfaları Yönetimi, meaning Internet Pages Management) is a proprietary CMS developed by and for the Turkish Ministry of Interior, used to manage official government websites for governorships, district governorships, and related agencies across Turkey.
ISAY.summary_abuse: ISAY is a government-internal CMS used exclusively for Turkish state websites. It has no consumer-facing surface, no public signup, and is not accessible outside of official government operations. There is no known abuse profile associated with this system.

iScripts.description: iScripts is a multi product CMS system based on PHP.
iScripts.history: iScripts is a US-based PHP CMS and script provider founded in the early 2000s, offering multi-product web scripts for community sites, classifieds, and social networks.
iScripts.summary_abuse: iScripts is a niche PHP CMS script vendor. Its scripts can be used to build social or classified platforms that could be misused, but no notable abuse incidents are documented.

iSET.description: iSET is an ecommerce platform providing tools to start, grow, and scale online stores.
iSET.history: iSET is a Brazilian e-commerce platform providing tools for online store creation and management. It has served the Brazilian market for many years.
iSET.summary_abuse: iSET is a Brazilian e-commerce SaaS with no documented notable abuse cases.

iSina Chat.description: iSina Chat is a live chat service that provides online support and FAQ for customers.
iSina Chat.history: iSina Chat is a live chat and FAQ support service operated by iSina agency with no notable ownership changes or public history available.
iSina Chat.summary_abuse: iSina Chat has no notable abuse signal. It is a small live chat tool with limited public presence and no documented abuse history.

Isotope.description: Isotope.js is a JavaScript library that makes it easy to sort, filter, and add Masonry layouts to items on a webpage.
Isotope.history: Isotope is a JavaScript filter and sort layout library created by David DeSandro of Metafizzy, building on his earlier Masonry layout library. Released around 2011, it uses dual licensing: GPLv3 for open source projects and a commercial license for paid use. It remains independently maintained by DeSandro.
Isotope.summary_abuse: Isotope is a small open-source JavaScript layout library with no hosted service and no abuse signal.

Isso.description: Isso is a lightweight commenting server written in Python and JavaScript, referred to as "Ich schrei sonst" in German.
Isso.history: Isso is a lightweight self-hosted commenting system written in Python with a SQLite backend, created as a privacy-respecting alternative to Disqus. It was initially developed by Martin Zimmermann and later maintained by community contributors under the isso-comments GitHub organization. It is MIT-licensed and stores all data locally.
Isso.summary_abuse: Isso is a self-hosted commenting system with full operator control. It has no hosted SaaS surface and no abuse tracking infrastructure, meaning operators must manage spam themselves. No notable abuse incidents are documented.

Issuu.description: Issuu is a digital discovery and publishing platform.
Issuu.history: Founded in Copenhagen in 2006 by Michael and Rubyn Bjerg Hansen, Mikkel Jensen, and Martin Ferro-Thomsen. Named one of Time's 50 Best Websites in 2009. Moved HQ to Palo Alto in 2013. Raised $51.3M total. Acquired by Bending Spoons (Italian mobile app company) on July 19, 2024.
Issuu.summary_abuse: Issuu is a digital publishing platform allowing users to upload and share documents publicly. Due to its free tier with publicly accessible documents, it has been used to distribute pirated content, spam-filled e-books, and phishing lure documents. The platform has received DMCA takedown requests and implements content moderation, but the sheer volume of uploads limits enforcement effectiveness.

It'seeze.description: It’seeze is a website design platform with a custom CMS for seamless site updates and management.
It'seeze.history: it'seeze was founded in 2003 in the UK by Peter Campbell, originally as a print company called Spoton.net, later evolving into a franchise-based web design and hosting company for UK and Irish small businesses. The franchise model has created a network of local consultants providing web design services.
It'seeze.summary_abuse: it'seeze is a UK franchise website builder for small businesses with no notable abuse signal. As a website hosting provider, its platform could host low-quality or spammy sites, but no documented patterns of systematic abuse are known.

Italiaonline.description: Italiaonline is a web platform that provides tools and services for building and managing websites.
Italiaonline.history: Italiaonline traces its roots to the Italian web portals Libero.it and Virgilio.it. The modern company was formed in February 2013 through the merger of Matrix S.p.A. and Libero S.r.l. Converted to S.p.A. status in April 2014. It is Italy's leading internet platform and the number one email service in Italy, generating over €100M in annual revenue.
Italiaonline.summary_abuse: Italiaonline operates the major Italian web portals Libero.it and Virgilio.it which host free email and web services. Free email and web hosting services from Italian portals have historically been used for spam campaigns, and Libero.it addresses in particular have appeared in phishing and spam reports, though the platform does implement AUP enforcement.

iTCHYROBOT.description: iTCHYROBOT is a platform that develops school websites and provides marketing strategies to support communication, branding, and digital engagement for educational institutions.
iTCHYROBOT.history: iTCHYROBOT is a UK-based CMS platform specializing in school websites and digital marketing for educational institutions.
iTCHYROBOT.summary_abuse: iTCHYROBOT is a niche UK school website platform with no documented abuse cases.

Iterable.description: Iterable is a cross-channel marketing platform that powers unified customer experiences.
Iterable.history: Founded in San Francisco in 2013 by Andrew Boni and Justin Zhu (ex-Twitter) after seeing the difficulty marketers had with email personalization. Raised $343M across 10 rounds from investors including Silver Lake, Viking Global, Capital One, and CRV. Reached a $2B valuation. Current CEO is Sam Allen.
Iterable.summary_abuse: Iterable is an enterprise cross-channel marketing automation platform. Mass email platforms like Iterable could be used to send spam if account controls fail, but Iterable enforces anti-spam policies and requires business verification. No specific documented abuse incidents are known.

Iteras.description: Iteras is a subscriber management system that organizes user accounts, tracks subscription activity, and supports controlled access to digital services.
Iteras.history: Iteras was founded in 2007 and provides subscription management software tailored for Nordic media companies including newspapers, magazines, and digital media. The platform is built and hosted entirely in Europe. No major ownership changes documented.
Iteras.summary_abuse: Iteras is a B2B subscription management platform for media companies with no notable abuse signal.

Iterate.description: Iterate is a customer insights manager (CIM) system, facilitating website and email surveys to harness customer insights across your entire business.
Iterate.history: Iterate was founded in 2016 in New York as a lightweight customer insights and survey platform designed to help startups gather user feedback via website, email, and in-app surveys. The company operates independently with public pricing starting from a free tier and paid plans up to $99/month. No notable funding rounds, acquisitions, or rebranding events are documented.
Iterate.summary_abuse: Iterate is a survey and customer insights tool. Survey platforms can occasionally be used to send phishing links via survey invitation emails, but Iterate's small scale and developer-focused positioning limit meaningful abuse exposure. No specific incidents are documented.

iThemes Security.description: iThemes Security(formerly known as Better WP Security) plugin enhances the security and protection of your WordPress website.
iThemes Security.history: iThemes Security (originally Better WP Security) is a WordPress security plugin founded around 2008. iThemes was acquired by Liquid Web in 2018 and subsequently rebranded as SolidWP in 2023 as part of the StellarWP brand umbrella.
iThemes Security.summary_abuse: iThemes Security is a WordPress security plugin with no notable abuse signal. As a defensive tool it is not typically exploited by attackers, though outdated installations of any WordPress plugin can be a vulnerability vector.

Itoris.description: Itoris is a developer specializing in widgets for ecommerce platforms, creating tools that enhance online store functionality.
Itoris.history: ITORIS Inc. is a Canadian Magento extension developer, founded in 2007 and based in Dundas, Ontario. The company has developed open-source extensions for Magento 1.x and Magento 2.x for over 15 years. No major ownership changes documented.
Itoris.summary_abuse: Itoris is a Magento extension marketplace with no notable abuse signal. It provides legitimate e-commerce software tools for merchants.

iubenda.description: iubenda is a compliance software used by businesses for their websites and apps.
iubenda.history: iubenda was founded in 2011 in Bologna, Italy, as a compliance software platform helping websites generate legally compliant privacy policies and cookie banners. The company grew to serve over 150,000 clients globally. In recent years iubenda joined forces with team.blue, a European web services group, while retaining operational independence.
iubenda.summary_abuse: iubenda is a compliance and cookie-consent tool with no notable abuse signal. Its function as a legal compliance helper means it is typically deployed by legitimate businesses seeking regulatory adherence, and it carries no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

iugu.description: iugu is a financial management platform that enables businesses to handle payments, billing, and invoicing through a unified system.
iugu.history: iugu is a Brazilian fintech platform founded around 2012, providing payment processing, billing, and invoicing infrastructure for businesses in Brazil. It serves the Brazilian market as a Stripe-like payments API.
iugu.summary_abuse: iugu is a Brazilian payment platform. Payment processors face fraud and chargebacks as standard operational risks, but iugu has no documented specific abuse incidents.

Ivory Search.description: Ivory Search is a WordPress search plugin that improves WordPress search by providing advanced options to extend search or exclude specific content from search.
Ivory Search.history: Ivory Search is a WordPress plugin that replaces the default WordPress search with advanced filtering and exclusion options. It is distributed via the WordPress.org plugin repository.
Ivory Search.summary_abuse: Ivory Search is a WordPress search enhancement plugin with no notable abuse signal. It improves site search functionality and has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would exploit.

iWeb.description: iWeb is a web site creation tool.
iWeb.history: iWeb was a website creation application developed by Apple, first released in 2006 as part of the iLife suite. Apple discontinued iWeb in 2011 and shut down the MobileMe hosting service in 2012, effectively ending the product.
iWeb.summary_abuse: iWeb was a discontinued Apple desktop application for creating websites. It has no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

iWiki.description: iWiki is a Dutch-based content management system provider, formerly known as Kirra.
iWiki.history: iWiki is a Dutch CMS platform, formerly known as Kirra, operated by the Dutch agency iwink.nl; no notable ownership changes or deprecation events beyond the rebrand are documented.
iWiki.summary_abuse: iWiki is a Dutch CMS platform with no notable abuse signal.

iyzico.description: iyzico is a payment receipt system management platform that offers ePayment solutions.
iyzico.history: iyzico was founded in 2013 in Istanbul, Turkey as an online payment solutions provider. PayU (owned by Naspers/Prosus) acquired iyzico for 65 million in June 2019, making it a key part of PayU's Turkish operations. iyzico serves major merchants including Amazon, Nike, H&M and Zara in Turkey.
iyzico.summary_abuse: iyzico is a legitimate Turkish payment platform owned by PayU. Payment processors face fraud and chargebacks as standard risks, but iyzico operates under PayU's compliance standards with no documented specific abuse incidents.

Izooto.description: iZooto is a user engagement and retention tool that leverages web push notifications to help business to drive repeat traffic, leads and sales.
Izooto.history: iZooto was founded in 2016 in Noida, India by Vivek Khandelwal, Neel Kothari, and Sachin Grover. It is a web and mobile push notification platform targeting publishers and digital media companies. It has grown to serve 15,000+ businesses across 100+ countries with $1M+ ARR, funded with only $100K from 17 investors.
Izooto.summary_abuse: iZooto is a web push notification platform used by publishers. Push notification services are frequently abused by scammy websites to send spam notifications or lure users to malicious pages. Sites running iZooto may push unwanted promotional or misleading notifications. The platform targets publishers so the abuse risk depends heavily on the site operator rather than iZooto directly.
